features.name: Swift Response
features.description: You learn to react with swift and decisive action in combat. You ignore difficult terrain, and you gain a rank of Mastery in Initiative. You gain another rank of Mastery at 9th level.

features.name: Lethal Precision
features.description: Choose one between Taijutsu and Bukijutsu. You can cast the chosen jutsu type using Dexterity in place of Strength for all calculations. You cannot switch this choice later.

features.name: Lethal Attack
features.description:
Once per turn, you can deal extra damage to a creature you score a successful attack against. The extra damage is a number of d8s equal to the Lethal Attack column of the Hunter-Nin class table (1d8 at level 1, scaling up to 7d8 at level 20).

Triggers only if: you have advantage on the attack roll, OR one of your allies is within 5 feet of the target.

If the triggering attack is a weapon attack, roll only half the listed dice (rounded down, minimum 1).

features.name: Cunning Action
features.description:
Your quick thinking and agility allow you to move and act quickly. When you attempt to Conceal an object, Hide, or Sneak, you gain a +2 bonus to your Dexterity (Stealth) check.

While sneaking, you move at your full movement speed, and you can reuse your last Hide check result instead of rolling again each time you sneak.

As a bonus action, you can take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.

features.name: Primary Target
features.description:
When rolling initiative, you can select any creature you can see within 60 feet and mark it as your Primary Target at no action cost. As a bonus action you can mark another creature within range. You can only have one Primary Target at a time; marks last until the creature reaches 0 HP or you mark another.

While marked, you have advantage on Wisdom checks made to track or search for the target so long as they are within the same country's borders, and you become aware of ONE of the following (refreshable on short rest):
  • one damage immunity they have
  • one damage resistance they have
  • their current Damage Reduction value

features.name: Hunter's Exploits
features.description: You learn two exploits (listed at the end of the Hunter-Nin chapter). You learn an additional exploit at 10th and 17th level. You can swap a learned exploit each time you complete a long rest.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description:
Increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat you qualify for, and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. You can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

You gain this feature again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level.

features.name: Uncanny Dodge
features.description: When a creature that you can see would deal damage to you, you can use your reaction to halve the triggering attack, jutsu, or effect's damage against you.

features.name: Mastery
features.description:
Choose any one skill or tool kit you have proficiency in. You gain a rank of Mastery in the chosen skill or tool kit.

You gain an additional Mastery choice at 10th and 15th level.

features.name: Hunted Target
features.description:
A creature marked as your Primary Target is counted as Hunted against you.

Hunted: a Hunted creature is being stalked by you and is on its last leg. When a Hunted creature would cast a jutsu that requires Hand Signs (HS), or Stand from prone, they trigger an attack of opportunity from you.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Second ASI/Feat. See the level-4 description.

features.name: Swift Response (upgrade)
features.description: You gain another rank of Mastery in Initiative (on top of the rank from level 1).

features.name: Hunter's Exploits (2)
features.description: You learn an additional exploit (total now 3).

features.name: Mastery (2)
features.description: Select another skill or tool kit you have proficiency in and gain a rank of Mastery in it.

features.name: Defensive Tactics
features.description:
You learn how to fight defensively. Choose ONE of the following; you gain a second choice at 17th level.
  • Escaping Danger — attacks of opportunity and reaction-triggered attacks against you are made at disadvantage.
  • Unbroken Will — advantage on saving throws to resist any Mental or Sensory Condition.
  • Evasion — when subjected to an effect that allows a Dex save for half damage, you take no damage on a success and only half on a failure.

features.name: Hunted Target (2) — Hunter's Strike
features.description: You gain a special reaction called Hunter's Strike. This reaction can only be used to make an attack of opportunity.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Third ASI/Feat. See the level-4 description.

features.name: Elusive
features.description: You are so evasive that attackers rarely gain the upper hand against you. No attack roll has advantage against you while you aren't incapacitated.

features.name: Mastery (3)
features.description: Select another skill or tool kit you have proficiency in and gain a rank of Mastery in it.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Fourth ASI/Feat. See the level-4 description.

features.name: Hunter's Exploits (3)
features.description: You learn an additional exploit (total now 4).

features.name: Defensive Tactics (2)
features.description: Choose a second Defensive Tactics option (see the level-11 entry).

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Fifth and final ASI/Feat. See the level-4 description.

features.name: Assassinate
features.description: You have mastered the art of hunting, tracking, and assassinating targets. You can trigger Lethal Attack against up to two creatures per turn, once each.

subclasses.name: Blade Warden
subclasses.description: Hunter-Nin who seek to master weapons and remove foes from the equation. Specialized fighting techniques for use against the direst threats, from an onslaught of enemies to towering brutes.

features.name: Warden's Proficiency
features.description:
You gain proficiency in Athletics and Intimidation; you can make checks with either using Strength or Dexterity. You also gain proficiency in all Blade-group weapons.

Choose one weapon type you're proficient with that doesn't have Two-Handed or Heavy — this becomes your Warden Weapon. It gains one property from the Warden Weapon Property table (Flurry Attack, Mortal, or Fatal). You can swap the property on any rest. At 10th level you may select a second property.

features.name: Blade's Prey
features.description:
You can trigger Lethal Attack with weapon attacks from your Warden Weapon twice per turn against the same creature.

You also gain the Warden's Assault Hunter Exploit for free (doesn't count against your Exploit-known limit).

features.name: Aggressive Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. When rolling initiative, you may use your Intimidation bonus as your Initiative bonus.

features.name: Warden's Presence
features.description: Your presence is passively intimidating to any creature not allied with you. Out of combat, you can substitute Intimidation for Deception or Persuasion checks. Creatures making Wisdom or Charisma checks against you or an ally suffer a -2 penalty.

features.name: Blade's Aggression
features.description: When you hit a creature with a Warden Weapon attack and use a Combat Exploit that would forgo two or more Lethal Attack dice, you reduce the number of dice forgone by 1.

features.name: Warden's Retaliation
features.description: If your Primary Target forces you to make a saving throw, you can use your reaction to make one weapon attack against them before resolving the save. On a hit, you gain advantage on that saving throw.

features.name: Superior Offense
features.description: You gain 30 feet of blindsight. Attacks you make cannot be made at disadvantage and are not subject to jutsu-based penalties.

subclasses.name: Necrotic Hand
subclasses.description: Hunters who follow an offensive Medical teaching, using necrosis to fight off enemies with cold-eyed, surgical efficiency.

features.name: Medical Proficiency
features.description:
You gain proficiency in Medicine and Medicine Kits; you can make checks with either using Intelligence or Wisdom. You can learn and add jutsu with the Medical keyword to your known-jutsu list.

Your Lethal Attack can be triggered against creatures with ranks of Envenomed or Corroded.

You also gain the Festering Siphonage Hunter Exploit for free.

features.name: Decaying Compound
features.description:
You carry a Black Medicine Array of 4 ampoules, refreshed on any rest. Spend an ampoule to Infuse a Poison/Necrotic jutsu you cast (triggering an effect from the Decaying Compound table) or to Putrefy a weapon you wield for 1 minute.

While putrefied, your weapon deals necrotic or poison damage, and once per turn when your attack result is 5+ over the target's AC you inflict one of the table effects (Cell Failure, Overdose, Sepsis, Viral).

features.name: Anatomical Studies
features.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage, you may forgo 1 or 2 Lethal Attack dice. Forgoing 1 gives the target 1 rank of Weakened; forgoing 2 makes the target's next saving throw against another creature at disadvantage (before your next turn).

features.name: Clinical Appraisal
features.description: You can use Medicine in place of Insight for See Through Lies or Sense Motive. After observing a creature for 1 minute, make a contested Medicine / Perception vs their Deception — on success, learn if they're conditioned/diseased and one of: drug/chemical influence, clan/heritage, or vulnerability.

features.name: Twisted Medicine
features.description:
You can refresh 2 ampoules of your Black Medicine Array by spending 10 minutes working on them. You learn two new uses:
  • Rejuvenation (bonus action) — feed an ampoule to a touched willing creature to heal 4d10 HP (once per short rest per target).
  • Suppression (action) — throw an ampoule at a point within 30 ft creating a 20-ft noxious mist; CON save vs 19 or your Ninjutsu save DC; on fail, creatures become Suppressed.

features.name: Mortal Wound
features.description: A creature with a Physical or Mental condition glows a soft red to you only. You gain Blindsight against such creatures within 60 feet of you.

features.name: Dr. Death
features.description: Your medical assassination talents are legendary. D-Rank Medical jutsu that make an attack cost 0 Chakra when you cast them. C-Rank Medical jutsu that make an attack cost 5 Chakra.

subclasses.name: Grave Stalker
subclasses.description: Consumed by the thrill of the hunt, these Hunter-Nin become whispers in the dark — hunting via camouflage, infiltration, and shadow-strikes.

features.name: Stalker's Proficiency
features.description:
You gain proficiency in Sleight of Hand and Deception, usable with Dexterity or Charisma. You gain 20 ft of Blindsight, and in dim light or darkness you are imperceptible to Blindsight or Tremor sense.

Your Primary Target becomes Stalked: -2 penalty on Perception/Insight checks against you, and if they're isolated (no allies within 10 ft) and you Lethal Attack them, roll +1 Lethal Attack die.

Lethal Attack can trigger against creatures with Demoralized or Dazzled ranks.

You gain the Shadow Step Hunter Exploit for free.

features.name: Shadow Stalker
features.description: You gain a Dread Arsenal of charges equal to your proficiency bonus (refreshes on long rest, regain up to 2 on short rest). As a bonus action, spend a charge to summon a Dread Weapon (lasts 1 min): d6 damage, psychic or necrotic (your choice), has Finesse and Light. It's valid for Shinobi Basics and Blade-group Bukijutsu.

features.name: Marked for Death
features.description: While wielding your Dread Weapon, when you cast a Ninjutsu or Genjutsu that forces a save, spend 1 Arsenal charge to impose -2 on the save. Against a Stalked target the save is made at disadvantage instead.

features.name: Master Ambusher
features.description:
You can use Smoke Bombs as a bonus action. When you and allies start combat hidden/unseen, you can use your Stealth bonus as everyone's initiative. Starting combat outside of stealth: advantage on initiative rolls.

Once every 10 minutes you can interject during a failed social check within 30 ft — contested Deception vs their Insight to reset the conversation.

features.name: One with the Darkness
features.description:
Your Blindsight increases to 30 ft. In dim light or darkness, you're imperceptible to Chakra sight and Darkvision.

When you deal Lethal Attack damage, you may forgo 1 Lethal Attack die to inflict 2 ranks of Dazzled on the creature.

features.name: A Walking Shadow
features.description:
While hidden from a Stalked creature, you gain a +2 bonus to your critical threat range. You also gain one of:
  • Fade Step (bonus action, 1 Arsenal charge) — teleport into a creature's shadow within 30 ft.
  • Silent Execution — when you drop a creature to 0 HP, spend 1 Arsenal charge to gain 30 ft movement and make 1 extra weapon attack with your Dread Weapon; can trigger Lethal Attack again if attacking another creature.

features.name: Shadow Assassination
features.description: When attacking a Stalked creature in Darkness, roll an extra d20 with the attack and take the highest. If you have disadvantage, you instead take the middle of the three dice.

subclasses.name: Undertaker
subclasses.description: Brutal, imposing, impossible to ignore — relentless juggernauts trained to break bones, crush morale, and shatter resistance.

features.name: Brutal Proficiency
features.description:
You gain proficiency in Athletics and Demolitions Kits, usable with Strength or Dexterity. You gain +10 ft movement speed and learn the Great Weapon Stance (Bukijutsu).

Lethal Attack can trigger against creatures with ranks of Bruised or Weakened.

You gain the Savage Attack Hunter Exploit for free.

features.name: Carnage
features.description:
When you roll initiative, you enter Executioner's Drive, lasting until the end of the encounter. While in it, once per turn when you deal or take damage, you gain 1 Carnage (max 3). Spend Carnage on:
  • Guillotine Cleave (1) — a melee weapon attack hits everyone in your weapon's range; Lethal Attack applies to each.
  • Butcher's Momentum (1) — on a hit, gain 30 ft movement and make 1 more weapon attack.
  • Casket Breaker (1) — after scoring less damage than rolled, make 1 more weapon attack that ignores up to 6 DR.

features.name: Savage Inflection
features.description: Once per turn when you spend a Carnage, reduce the Lethal Damage die cost of a Combat Exploit by 1.

features.name: Unspoken Threat
features.description: When a non-hostile creature makes a CHA or WIS check against you or an ally, you gain 1 Social Carnage (max 1, expires in 1 min). Spend it to grant advantage on your or an ally's responding CHA/WIS check.

features.name: Weight of Violence
features.description:
You gain two new Carnage spends:
  • Demonic Aura (2) — at turn start, until your next turn's end, creatures targeting you with attacks or jutsu suffer your Lethal Attack if you strike them, even past your Lethal Attack use limit.
  • Murderous Aura (2) — at turn start, creatures within 15 ft make a Wisdom save vs your Taijutsu or Ninjutsu DC; on failure, 2 ranks of Demoralized.

features.name: The Butcher's Promise
features.description: When your Primary Target triggers an attack of opportunity, you make it at advantage. If using your Hunter's Strike reaction, on a hit the target makes a Strength save vs your Taijutsu save DC or is knocked prone. If already standing from prone, they stay prone.

features.name: Harvest
features.description: Red Harvest. When you deal Weapon Damage, spend 3 Carnage: treat the weapon damage as maximum, trigger Lethal Attack ignoring requirements, and the target makes a Constitution save vs the triggering attack/damage save DC. On success they're knocked prone; on failure they're Staggered, have disadvantage on their next save from another creature, and are knocked prone.

subclasses.name: Void Walker
subclasses.description: Hunters who weaponize Time & Space ninjutsu, specializing in hit-and-run tactics — teleporting in and out of combat as easily as blinking.

features.name: Void Proficiency
features.description:
You gain proficiency in Ninshou (usable with Dex or Int) and reduce Ninjutsu-scroll crafting downtime by 2 (if reduced to 0, craft over a long rest).

When you or a creature you can see teleports within 10 ft of one another, you can trigger Lethal Attack ignoring its requirements.

Lethal Attack can trigger against creatures with ranks of Sealed.

You gain the Void Step Hunter Exploit for free.

features.name: Void Anchor
features.description:
As a bonus action, place a Void Anchor on a creature or surface/object within 60 ft. Up to 4 Anchors at once, each lasting 1 minute (oldest drops if you go over the cap). If you teleport within 10 ft of an Anchored creature, make a weapon attack as a bonus action.

When Lethal Attack hits a marked creature you may forgo 1 or 2 Lethal Attack dice to use:
  • Flash Step (1 die) — teleport up to 30 ft to an unoccupied space you see.
  • Displacement Strike (2 dice) — target makes a Strength save vs your Ninjutsu/Taijutsu save DC; on failure, teleported into/adjacent to another Anchored creature.

features.name: Void Sense
features.description: Anchors placed outside combat last 10 minutes. You can see and hear through them as if standing there (within 30 ft of the Anchor). Chakra- or jutsu-based effects within 30 ft of an Anchor cause it to hum for you.

features.name: Vorpal Strike
features.description:
When marking a creature, you can choose to Layer the Anchor (counts as two active Anchors). A Layered creature lets you use:
  • Vorpal Passthrough — when casting an attack-roll jutsu or taking Attack action, teleport adjacent to the double-anchored creature as part of that action.
  • Reverse Summoning (Action) — force the creature to Strength save vs your save DC; failure summons them adjacent, both seals dispel; success breaks one Anchor.

features.name: Sealing Arts
features.description: Within 60 ft of a creature or chakra-based effect you see/are aware of, spend an Action to place 3 Anchors and make a Ninshou check vs the effect's save DC (or rank-based DC: D 10, C 14, B 18, A 22, S 26). On success, the effect is dispelled. 10-minute cooldown.

features.name: Vorpal Blade
features.description: Mark one weapon with your Void Anchor. When Lethal Attack triggers against the target, your attack bypasses any structure, construct, or barrier that would intercept damage. On a critical hit with the anchored weapon, you may use Flash Step or Displacement Strike without forgoing any Lethal Attack dice.

features.name: Vorpal Technique
features.description: As an Action, connect all your Void Anchors in a web and teleport to each simultaneously. All creatures within 10 ft of each chosen Anchor become targets. Make one melee Taijutsu or Ninjutsu attack, compared to each target's AC. On hit, deal Weapon/Unarmed Damage and trigger Lethal Attack against each hit creature. Crits only apply to one. All Anchors dispel; you reappear within 30 ft of one hit creature.

subclasses.name: Wolves Legacy
subclasses.description: Forged by surviving hardship — scarred and reconstructed, these operatives carry prosthetic gauntlets housing deadly ninja tools and fight by reading their enemies' rhythm.

features.name: Wolf's Proficiency
features.description:
You gain proficiency in Martial Arts and Survival, usable with Strength or Dexterity. Your technique is represented as Posture — once per turn when you reduce/resist damage with a class feature or reaction jutsu, gain 1 Posture (max 3, lasts 1 min). With or without Posture, you can:
  • Deflect (reaction) — reduce triggering damage by 4, or 10 with 1 Posture.
  • Parry (reaction vs crit) — resist the triggering damage, or all damage until your next turn with 1 Posture.
  • Retaliate (reaction) — make an attack on the damager if in weapon range, or don't spend your reaction by paying 1 Posture.

Lethal Attack can trigger against creatures with Dazed or Confused ranks.

You gain the Posture Break Hunter Exploit for free.

features.name: Shinobi's Prosthetic
features.description: You have a custom prosthetic arm/gauntlet with 2 Prosthetic Slots (start with 2 tools from the table; add one at 7th, 10th, and 14th level). Tool attack/save DCs use your Taijutsu or Ninjutsu (your choice). Prosthetic tools can be used no more than 4 times per 10 minutes.

features.name: Shinobi's Flow State
features.description:
Each ability granted by Wolf's Proficiency gains an enhanced option:
  • Deflect — 2 Posture reduces triggering and subsequent damage by 10 until end of turn.
  • Parry — 2 Posture makes resistance last until your next turn and can apply to a willing ally within weapon range.
  • Retaliate — 2 Posture lets you cast a 1-action Bukijutsu instead of attacking (still costs reaction).

features.name: Eyes of a Shinobi
features.description: Shinobi's Awareness (bonus action) — make a Survival check; for 1 minute you sense when a creature is observing/targeting you even through stealth, invisibility, or extended means. Creatures within 60 ft holding weapons, preparing to attack, or in combat stress alert this sense. While active, you can't be surprised and have no initiative penalties.

features.name: Wolf Techniques
features.description: Once per turn, spend 2 Posture to use one of your Prosthetic Tools at no action cost. When a Prosthetic Tool deals damage or requires an attack roll, spend 1 Posture to trigger Lethal Attack against all affected creatures (as long as you haven't used it yet that turn).

features.name: Mortal Rhythm
features.description: Hunter's Strike can be used as part of any reaction granted by Wolf's Proficiency. If you use it with Deflect/Parry/Retaliate, spend 2 Posture: the triggering creature makes a Constitution save vs your Taijutsu/Ninjutsu save DC; on failure their action is Interrupted. By 5 or more, also Dazed and you gain 1 Posture.

features.name: Kage's Die Twice
features.description:
Once per full rest, when reduced to 0 HP you can instead stay standing at 1 HP, gain 3 Posture, and regain your reaction if spent.

You also gain the Shadow Execution Hunter Exploit.

pickable.editorDescription: Hunter-Nin exploits, learned via the level-up window. Use + Custom for homebrew entries.
pickable.pickerDescription: Hunter's Exploits are learned at levels 3 (×2), 10 (+1), and 17 (+1). Free subclass exploits are added automatically and don't count against this pick.

options.name: Aim
options.description: As a bonus action, make a Perception check vs the target's AC. On success, the first attack made against the target is at advantage. If you beat the AC by 10 or more, that attack's critical threat range is increased by +1.

options.name: Analyze
options.description: As a bonus action, make an Investigation check contested by the target's Deception. On success, learn two traits the adversary has; if they're at or below your level, also learn their highest save bonus. A creature can only be Analyzed once per rest.

options.name: Botany
options.description: When you finish a long rest, choose two regions (Land of Fire, Earth, Lightning, Water, Wind, Snow, or three Minor Lands). Until your next full rest, you gain a rank of Mastery to all Nature and Survival checks made within the chosen lands.

options.name: Confuse Beast
options.description: As an action, pick a beast within 30 ft. Make an Animal Handling check contested by its Wisdom save. On success, the beast cannot take actions or reactions until the end of your next turn.

options.name: Diplomacy
options.description: As an action, convince a creature within 30 ft who can hear and understand you. Contested Persuasion vs their Insight (they get advantage if you damaged them in the last hour). On success, increase their disposition toward you — they may help you with a goal against their better judgment.

options.name: Distraction
options.description: As a reaction on an ally's turn, target a creature within 30 ft that can hear you. Make a Deception check contested by the target's Wisdom save. On success, the target becomes Confused.

options.name: Emulate Predator
options.description: While hidden from a creature within 60 ft, as an action make a Nature check contested by its Wisdom save. On success, the target is Demoralized and must Dash away on its next turn via the safest available route, unless nowhere to go.

options.name: False Faces
options.description: As an action, make a Performance check contested by observers' Insight. On success, perfectly imitate a humanoid you've interacted with for at least 10 minutes. Disguise lasts 1 hour. Doesn't duplicate Kekkei Genkai, chakra signature, or fool jutsu-based sensory effects.

options.name: Hinder
options.description: As a reaction to an attack or jutsu cast within 30 ft, make a Sleight of Hand check contested by the target's Constitution save. On success, the target becomes Dazzled.

options.name: I Wouldn't Do That
options.description: As a reaction when a creature would become hostile in a social encounter, make an Intimidation check contested by their Wisdom save. On failure, they're Demoralized and seek another way to resolve things. If others become hostile, the hesitated creature acts last in initiative and doesn't roll.

options.name: Intuit
options.description: As an action, target a creature within 30 ft. Make an Insight check contested by their Deception. On success, gain the benefit of See Through Lies and Sense Motive against that creature.

options.name: Lustful Assumptions
options.description: In a social encounter with a non-negative/non-hostile creature, as an action make a Persuasion check contested by their Insight. On success, the target is Charmed by you for 1 hour.

options.name: Kleptomaniac
options.description: As a bonus action, mark a lock or creature as your Primary Target. While marked this way, the DC to pick the lock or pickpocket the creature is reduced by 4.

options.name: Shadow Step
options.description: While in dim light, darkness, or obscured, as a bonus action teleport to another space granting the same condition. Make a Stealth check vs every hostile creature's Passive Perception; on success, you become undetectable by sensory-keyword jutsu until the end of your next turn.

options.name: Study
options.description: As a reaction when a creature attempts a jutsu or attack against you, make a History check contested by their Charisma save. On success, you have advantage on the first of either a skill check or a saving throw made against that creature before the end of your next turn.

options.name: Tumble
options.description: As a reaction to being attacked, make an Acrobatics check vs the attacker's attack roll result. On success, the attack reduces its damage by 2 damage dice.

options.name: Vanish
options.description: While being observed, as an action make a Stealth check contested by all creatures' Perception. On success, move up to your speed into a space with partial or total concealment and treat your result as a Hide attempt. Observers see you vanish into thin air.

options.name: Deceptive Exchange
options.description: In a social exchange, when a creature attempts to deceive you, as a reaction make a contested Deception check vs their result. On success, you glean one piece of truthful information based on the lie they tried to pass.

options.name: Owls Teachings
options.description: While observing a creature, as an action make a contested Insight vs their Deception. On success, see through them; if they have hostile intentions toward you or anyone near them, you become aware of their malice.

options.name: Void Step
options.description: By spending all your movement, select a Void Anchor you currently have active and teleport to it, landing in a space of your choice within 5 ft of the Anchor. If you teleport within 5 ft of a creature marked with your Anchor, your next attack against them can trigger Lethal Attack.

options.name: Battle Cry
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo 1 Lethal Attack die. The target and all hostile creatures within 10 ft of the target must make a Wisdom save. On failure, affected creatures gain 1 rank of Demoralized.

options.name: Brute
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo 1 Lethal Attack die. The target makes a Strength save. On failure, the target is knocked prone and gains 1 rank of Weakened.

options.name: Chakra Recoil
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo up to 2 Lethal Attack dice. Roll the forgone dice — gain temporary Chakra equal to the highest roll. Once every 10 minutes while in combat.

options.name: Feint
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage to a target within 30 ft, forgo 2 Lethal Attack dice. The target makes a Wisdom save. On failure, the target cannot target you until the end of their next turn. Once every 10 minutes per creature.

options.name: Festering Siphonage
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo 1 Lethal Attack die. The target makes a Strength save. On failure, the target reduces their AC and saving throws by 1 until the end of your next turn, and you gain a +2 bonus to your next attack against them before the end of your next turn.

options.name: Savage Attack
options.description:
When you deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo 1 or 2 Lethal Attack dice.
  • 1 die: the target makes a Strength save; on failure, they gain 1 rank of Weakened and have disadvantage on their next attack roll before your next turn.
  • 2 dice: the target makes a Strength save; on failure, they're knocked prone and cannot take reactions.

options.name: Instruct
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo 3 Lethal Attack dice. Each allied creature's next attack roll against the target is made at advantage.

options.name: Posture Break
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo 1 Lethal Attack die (or 0 dice if this exploit is used as part of your Retaliate). The damaged creature cannot cast jutsu that boost their AC or provide bonus DR until the end of your next turn.

options.name: Shadow Execution
options.description: When you score a critical hit and deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo 5 Lethal Attack dice. The affected creature makes a Constitution save. On failure, they become Stunned until the end of your next turn.

options.name: Sunder
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo 2 Lethal Attack dice. The target makes a Dexterity save. On failure, they reduce their AC by 4 until the end of their next turn.

options.name: Wardens Assault
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage using your Warden Weapon, forgo up to 3 Lethal Attack dice. You manifest afterimages, repeating your attack against other creatures of your choice in range — each hit deals the original damage plus the remaining Lethal Attack damage.

masteryGrants.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.

Choose any one skill or tool kit you have proficiency in. You gain a rank of Mastery in it.
Select another proficient skill or tool kit and gain a rank of Mastery in it.

features.name: Refined Focus
features.description:
You begin your path to mastery over Chakra and Ninjutsu. You carry Focus equal to 1 + proficiency bonus, spent on Moldings — feats of chakra control layered onto a Ninjutsu you cast (most cost 1 Focus; use one per jutsu). Spent Focus recovers on a long rest.

You start knowing 2 Moldings (see the class chart for how many you know at higher levels). Moldings only apply to Ninjutsu cast without the Combination keyword.

features.name: Chakra Compression
features.description:
Once per turn, when you cast a Ninjutsu as an Action, compress your chakra to conserve stamina — reduce the jutsu's cost by an amount equal to its rank: D-Rank 1, C-Rank 2, B-Rank 3, A-Rank 4, S-Rank 5.

At 11th level, you can spend 1 Focus to cast a non-Combination Ninjutsu with a 1-Action casting time at D-Rank (or lower) for 0 chakra. This becomes C-Rank or lower at 17th level.

features.name: Scroll Stance (Chakra Stance)
features.description: Choose one Chakra Stance from Chapter 13: Customization Options (track the chosen stance in Notes).

features.name: Ninjutsu Tradition
features.description: You commit to a Tradition (subclass) that shapes your Ninjutsu style. Your Tradition grants features at 2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th levels.

features.name: Genius
features.description: Your study of Ninshou and chakra theory earns you a rank of Mastery in the Ninshou skill. You gain an additional rank of Mastery in Ninshou at 7th and 13th levels.

features.name: Chakra Surge
features.description:
In combat, once per turn as a bonus action, enter one of two heightened battle states (you can't be in both at once): Crash or Flow. You can remain in a state for up to 1 minute combined; once you leave both states you can't re-enter for 10 minutes of uninterrupted rest or meditation.

CRASH (Will Amp)
Crash symbolizes you releasing the limits on your chakra coils, empowering the jutsu you cast.
  • Once per casting, you gain a +1d10 bonus to damage you deal with Ninjutsu you cast. Increases to +2d10 at 7th, 3d10 at 11th, 4d10 at 15th, and 5d10 at 19th level. Reduce the dice rolled by half (rounded up) if the jutsu affects an area.
  • You can use two or more Moldings at once as part of the same casting.
  • You can use the Draw Reserves skill action as an Action.
  • You cannot benefit from any source of cost reduction. Casting a Jutsu at 0 Cost does not count as cost reduction.

FLOW (Will Amp)
Flow symbolizes you controlling and refining your chakra, making slight adjustments to it as the battle progresses.
  • You gain a +1 bonus to your Ninjutsu Save DC. Increases to +2 at 11th level and +3 at 19th level.
  • When you begin your turn in this state, you regain 1 Focus.
  • You make Concentration checks to maintain Ninjutsu at advantage.
  • You cannot benefit from jutsu or features that boost the damage you deal with Ninjutsu.

actions.name: Draw Reserves
actions.description: While in Crash, you can take the Draw Reserves skill action as an Action.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description:
Increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat you qualify for, and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. Cannot increase a score above 20.

Repeats at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level.

features.name: Tenketsu Refinement
features.description: When you complete a short rest, if you have less than half of your maximum Focus, you set your Focus to half of its maximum value.

features.name: Meditation
features.description:
When you and allied creatures spend at least 10 uninterrupted minutes resting, you may lead a group meditation — roll 1d8 and regain the result as chakra for yourself and all participating allies. A creature can only benefit once per short rest.

Becomes 2d8 at 11th level and 3d8 at 17th level.

features.name: Counterseal
features.description:
When you or an allied creature within 30 ft would make a saving throw vs a Fuinjutsu-keyword jutsu (or against a Sealed condition), you can make the chosen ally's save at advantage.

At 13th level, you can instead spend 1 Focus to apply this to all willing creatures in a 30-ft radius centered on yourself.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Second ASI/Feat. See the level-4 description.

features.name: Perfected Surge
features.description:
Pick one Crash Evolution and one Flow Evolution (or a single Neutral Evolution that counts as either) — this grants the 9th-level benefit of the chosen evolution. At 15th level, pick one more evolution; it only grants its 15th-level benefit.

CRASH (Perfected) (Will Amp)
  • Once per casting, you gain a +5d10 bonus to damage you deal with Ninjutsu you cast.
  • You can use three or more Moldings at once as part of the same casting.
  • Your Ninjutsu treats immunity to their damage type like resistance.

FLOW (Perfected) (Will Amp)
  • You gain a +3 bonus to your Ninjutsu Save DC.
  • You regain 1 Focus at the start and end of each of your turns.
  • You can maintain concentration on up to 3 Ninjutsu at once.

features.name: Ninjutsu Tradition (3)
features.description: Your Tradition grants another feature at this level.

features.name: Chakra Compression (2)
features.description: You can now spend 1 Focus to cast a non-Combination Ninjutsu (1-Action cast time) at D-Rank for 0 chakra.

features.name: Meditation (2)
features.description: Meditation now rolls 2d8 chakra restored.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Third ASI/Feat. See the level-4 description.

features.name: Counterseal (2)
features.description: You can now spend 1 Focus to extend Counterseal's effect to all willing creatures in a 30-ft radius centered on yourself.

features.name: Perfected Surge (2)
features.description: Pick one more evolution for your Chakra Surge (you gain only its 15th-level benefit).

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Fourth ASI/Feat. See the level-4 description.

features.name: Chakra Compression (3)
features.description: The free-cast from Chakra Compression now applies to C-Rank or lower Ninjutsu.

features.name: Meditation (3)
features.description: Meditation now rolls 3d8 chakra restored.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Fifth and final ASI/Feat. See the level-4 description.

features.name: Ninjutsu Master
features.description: Your chakra has become boundless. When you would enter either your Crash or Flow state, you may spend 4 Focus to perfect it for the next minute. Once used, you cannot do so again for 10 minutes.

subclasses.name: Blaze Walker
subclasses.description: Primal engine of destruction when using Fire Release Ninjutsu. Burns hotter, faster, and brighter than others.

features.name: Fire Release
features.description: You gain the ability to learn Ninjutsu with the Fire Release keyword. If you already had access, learn 2 Fire Release ninjutsu you qualify for (one may be C-Rank or lower). You also learn/create Fire Release ninjutsu in half the required time.

features.name: Pyroclasm
features.description: Once per turn, when you cast a Fire Release jutsu, you gain 1 Pyroclasm (max 3). Spend to Detonate, Hellrend, or Devastation. (See PDF for full rules text.)

features.name: Over the Limit
features.description: Entering Crash while spending 1 Pyroclasm makes you Overheat for the duration. Fire Release jutsu you cast gain +2 damage (+4 at 10, +6 at 18). At end of turn take 1d6 fire damage (unreducible).

features.name: Crimson Kindling
features.description: You learn the Crimson Kindling Molding automatically (granted free).

features.name: Tidebreaker
features.description: Fire Release jutsu gain advantage on Clash checks vs Water Release.

features.name: Ignition
features.description: Spend 1 Focus (or 2) to enhance a Pyroclasm ability.

features.name: Over the Limit — Burnout
features.description: Spend 2 extra Focus when entering Overheat to become Burnout — gains 1 Pyroclasm/turn + Detonate as bonus action, but take 3d6 fire/turn.

features.name: Blaze Walker (Ultimate Molding)
features.description: You learn the Blaze Walker ultimate Molding (granted free).

subclasses.name: Lightning Breaker
subclasses.description: Unmatched force of unstoppable power, greater than a lightning storm, when using Lightning Release Ninjutsu.

features.name: Lightning Release
features.description: Gain access to Lightning Release; learn 2 if already have it. Halved crafting time.

features.name: Stormcircuit
features.description: Mark creatures failing saves vs your Lightning Release with Static; multiple Static creatures become Linked (shared effects via forgoing damage dice).

features.name: Voltaic
features.description: Enter Voltaic in Flow state by dispelling a Static mark. Immune to Shocked's effects, consume Shocked ranks on cast creatures, then spend them on Circumvent / Speed Jolt / Supercharge. At end of turn, 1d6 lightning per Shocked rank.

features.name: Gigawatt Pulse
features.description: You learn the Gigawatt Pulse Molding automatically (granted free).

features.name: Breeze Breaker
features.description: Lightning Release jutsu gain advantage on Clash checks vs Wind Release.

features.name: Galvanize
features.description: Additional Stormcircuit effects when forgoing damage dice (1 die → disadvantage on next attack; 2 → pull 20 ft; 3 → no bonus action next turn).

features.name: Voltaic — Conduction
features.description: Spend 2 extra Focus to enter Conduction instead. 1 Shocked rank at each turn start, Speed Jolt uncapped. Damage becomes 1d12 per rank.

features.name: Lightning Breaker (Ultimate Molding)
features.description: You learn the Lightning Breaker ultimate Molding (granted free).

subclasses.name: Stone Crusher
subclasses.description: Unwavering force of strength, tenacity, and mountain-like toughness when using Earth Release Ninjutsu.

features.name: Earth Release
features.description: Gain Earth Release; learn 2 if already have it. Halved crafting time. Earthen Artifacts can have AC 10 / 10 HP.

features.name: Seismic Tether
features.description: Place Geo-Tethers within 20 ft (max 2 → 3 → 4). On Earth Release cast or damage, gain 1 Tension (max 4). Spend on Stone Bind / Grit / Adamantine Blood.

features.name: Mountain's Aegis
features.description: Enter Flow + 1 Tension to manifest Aegis. Within 10 ft of an Earthen Artifact, redirect half damage to it. AC -1 per turn on damage.

features.name: Tectonic Mantle
features.description: You learn the Tectonic Mantle Molding automatically (granted free).

features.name: Current Breaker
features.description: Earth Release jutsu gain advantage on Clash checks vs Lightning Release.

features.name: Bulwark
features.description: Max Tension 6, constructs 20 HP, tethers 30 ft, free Stone Bind on hostile movement, ally-Grit redirection.

features.name: Mountain's Aegis — Bastion
features.description: 2 extra Focus → Bastion: immunity to Bruised/Dazed/Chilled for self + allies within 20 ft, AC reduction unlimited per turn to -3.

features.name: Stone Crusher (Ultimate Molding)
features.description: You learn the Stone Crusher ultimate Molding (granted free).

subclasses.name: Storm Terror
subclasses.description: A ruthless natural disaster and unmatched collateral destruction when using Wind Release Ninjutsu.

features.name: Wind Release
features.description: Gain Wind Release; learn 2 if already have it. Halved crafting time. Proficient with Gunbai Fan / Giant Iron Fan; they gain Evocation + Wind damage at 60 ft.

features.name: Tempest Pressure
features.description: On Wind Release cast or Ninjutsu attack with Evocation weapon, gain 1 Pressure (max 3, 1 min). +2 temp HP per Pressure at turn start. Spend on Gale Burst / Sharpening / Dragwind.

features.name: Storm Herald
features.description: Enter Crash + 1 Pressure → Herald: 40-ft fly speed, halve Crash damage bonus into two jutsu. But +1 to enemy attack rolls targeting you.

features.name: Gale Fracture
features.description: You learn the Gale Fracture Molding automatically (granted free).

features.name: Blaze Breaker
features.description: Wind Release jutsu gain advantage on Clash checks vs Fire Release.

features.name: Eye of the Storm
features.description: Max Pressure 5. Spend 1 Focus on enhanced versions of Gale Burst / Sharpening / Dragwind.

features.name: Storm Herald — Typhoon
features.description: 2 extra Focus → Typhoon: gain 1 Pressure per turn, free base Gale Burst/Sharpening/Dragwind once/turn. But enemies have +2 attack and +1 crit threat against you.

features.name: Storm Terror (Ultimate Molding)
features.description: You learn the Storm Terror ultimate Molding (granted free).

subclasses.name: Summoner
subclasses.description: Walks the path closest to Sage hood — known for the widest arrangement of jutsu learned directly from sage creatures.

features.name: Bonded Summoning
features.description: Establish a Sage contract and bond with your first summoned creature. Scales with you: highest rank at half cost, half HP/slot restore on rest, shares your level, etc.

features.name: Tag-Team
features.description: Role-based benefits when you and your Bonded fight together (Strikers/Caster/Controller/Defender/Lurker/Supporter).

features.name: Summoning Duality
features.description:
Enter Crash/Flow with 1 Focus — you and Bonded enter opposite states, with role-specific benefits. Bonded loses 2 slots/turn.

CRASH DUALITY (Will Amp)
  • Once per casting, you gain a +1d10 bonus to damage you deal with Ninjutsu you cast. Increases to +2d10 at 11th and +3d10 at 19th level.
  • You can use two or more Moldings at once as part of the same casting.
  • You can use the Draw Reserves skill action as an Action.
  • You do not benefit from cost reduction from any source. Casting a Jutsu at 0 Cost does not count as cost reduction.

FLOW DUALITY (Will Amp)
  • You gain a +1 bonus to your Ninjutsu Save DC. Increases to +2 at 15th level.
  • When you begin your turn in this state, you regain 1 Focus.
  • If you are a Bonded creature, you instead regain 1 Jutsu Slot.
  • You make Concentration checks to maintain Ninjutsu at advantage.
  • You cannot benefit from jutsu, features or traits that boost the damage you deal with Ninjutsu.

actions.name: Draw Reserves
actions.description: While in Crash Duality, you can take the Draw Reserves skill action as an Action.

features.name: Combination Technique
features.description: You learn the Combination Technique Molding automatically (granted free).

features.name: Rule Breaker
features.description: Cast Summoning Technique as a Bonus Action.

features.name: Summoning Expert
features.description: Gain a second Bonded creature (half your level, rounded up). Both can be summoned; Action or Reaction commands the one that hasn't acted this turn.

features.name: Summoning Master
features.description: Spend 4 Focus on summoning → Bonded gains a Bonded Role for 1 minute before auto-dispel.

features.name: Synchronization Technique
features.description: You learn the Synchronization Technique ultimate Molding (granted free).

subclasses.name: Tsunami
subclasses.description: Embodies the force of a tsunami — unphased by the sea's power and all-encompassing like an ocean when using Water Release Ninjutsu.

features.name: Water Release
features.description: Gain Water Release; learn 2 if already have it. Halved crafting time.

features.name: Shifting Tides
features.description: On Water Release cast or Evocation-weapon Ninjutsu attack, enter one of four stances (Shark Maw, Piranha Fang, Surge, Undertow) until your next turn. Swap once/turn.

features.name: Surging Maelstrom
features.description: Enter Crash + 1 Focus → Maelstrom: use two Shift Tides stances at once; once/turn on Cold damage with Water Release, inflict Tidal Draw. +2 per damage instance taken.

features.name: Pressure Stagnation
features.description: You learn the Pressure Stagnation Molding automatically (granted free).

features.name: Terra Breaker
features.description: Water Release jutsu gain advantage on Clash checks vs Earth Release.

features.name: Tidal Crescendo
features.description: Enhanced stances — Shark Maw full Cold → Chakra; Piranha Fang 2/turn; Surge 15 ft; Undertow all creatures in area.

features.name: Surging Maelstrom — Abyss
features.description: 2 extra Focus → Abyss: Tidal Draw on ALL Water Release targets, regain 1 Focus per Chakra-damage instance. But +4 damage per instance taken.

features.name: Tsunami (Ultimate Molding)
features.description: You learn the Tsunami ultimate Molding (granted free).

pickable.editorDescription: Refined Focus moldings, learned via the level-up window. Use + Custom for homebrew entries.
pickable.pickerDescription: Refined Focus Moldings are learned at levels 1 (×2), 4, 7, 10, 13, 16, and 19 (+1 each). Tradition-granted moldings are added automatically and don't count against this pick.

options.name: Careful Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu that forces other creatures to make a saving throw, select up to 4 creatures. Chosen creatures suffer no effect from the cast Ninjutsu.

options.name: Distant Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu, increase its range by +30 feet. If the Ninjutsu has a range of touch, the range instead becomes 30 feet.

options.name: Doubled Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu that targets only one creature and doesn't have a range of self, you can target a second creature in range with the same Ninjutsu.

options.name: Focused Ninjutsu
options.description: When you're forced to make a concentration check to maintain a Ninjutsu you've cast, you automatically pass. Can be used in addition to other moldings.

options.name: Heightened Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu that forces a saving throw, give one target disadvantage on its first saving throw against the Ninjutsu. Can be used in addition to other moldings.

options.name: Piercing Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu that deals damage, it ignores resistance and half of a creature's DR. Creatures immune to the damage type instead treat their immunity as resistance.

options.name: Potent Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu that deals damage, after damage is rolled you can select up to 3 damage dice and treat each selected die as the maximum possible result.

options.name: Precise Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu that requires an attack roll, you can gain advantage on one of the attacks.

options.name: Quickened Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu that has a casting time of 1 action, you can change the casting time to 1 bonus action.

options.name: Reactive Ninjutsu
options.description: As a reaction, on your turn, you can cast a Ninjutsu with a casting time of 1 action.

options.name: Redirected Ninjutsu
options.description: If you make an attack roll for a Ninjutsu and would miss, you can remake the attack roll targeting another creature within range. You must use the second result. Can be used with other moldings.

options.name: Subtle Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu, you can cast it without any hand signs. Can be used in addition to other moldings.

options.name: Tenacious Ninjutsu
options.description: When a creature attempts to Clash, dispel, or interrupt a Ninjutsu you're casting or have cast, use your reaction to increase your clash check by 1d10 and the DC of Countering, dispelling, or interrupting this jutsu by +2.

options.name: Widened Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu with an area of effect, increase its size (line +5/+10, cube/sphere +5 ft radius, cone +10 ft length, cylinder +10 ft radius or height).

options.name: Jutsu Breaker
options.description: When a creature within 90 feet casts a Ninjutsu that would deal damage, as a reaction cast a 1-action Ninjutsu you know. It gains the clash keyword if it doesn't have it and automatically initiates a clash.

options.name: Crimson Kindling
options.description: Area Fire Release jutsu: creatures with Burned ranks failing saves suffer -1 to their jutsu save DCs per rank of Burned. Plant/Beast/flammable Construct makes the save at disadvantage.

options.name: Gigawatt Pulse
options.description: On Lightning Release cast, all allies within 30 ft gain +2 to a chosen statistic until your next turn. Aberration/Celestial/Mutant enemies affected make all saves at disadvantage.

options.name: Tectonic Mantle
options.description: Allies within 20 ft of a Geo-Tether gain resistance to the next damage. Hostile creatures failing their save have speed 0 and disadvantage on next Dex save. Demon/Monstrosity/Undead → disadvantage on all saves.

options.name: Gale Fracture
options.description: On Wind Release cast, affected creatures failing the save suffer -2 AC and -1 saves until your next turn.

options.name: Combination Technique
options.description: Command your Bonded to spend their reaction to embolden the jutsu cast — upcast by 2 ranks, gains any Role/Special feature your summon qualifies for. Becomes C-Rank at 14th.

options.name: Pressure Stagnation
options.description: On Water Release cast, create a 40-ft Stagnation sphere. Hostile creatures casting Hand-Sign jutsu in it take 4d6 Cold+Chakra (6d6 at 14th).

options.name: Blaze Walker (Ultimate)
options.description: On Fire Release cast, affected creatures take +8 damage from other sources the first time each turn. Cannot lose/remove Burned ranks until your next turn.

options.name: Lightning Breaker (Ultimate)
options.description: On Lightning Release cast, affected creatures have all non-condition jutsu effects dispelled, then CON save or Stunned until end of their next turn.

options.name: Stone Crusher (Ultimate)
options.description: On Earth Release cast, allies within 30 ft of a Geo-Tether gain +4 AC and crit immunity. Hostile creatures within 30 ft of a Tether suffer Stone Bind's effects as if they failed.

options.name: Storm Terror (Ultimate)
options.description: On Wind Release cast, generate a 60-ft Storm Zone around one creature. Hostiles inside lose cover/concealment/Invisibility, have disadvantage on ranged attacks, and Barrier jutsu summon with half HP.

options.name: Synchronization Technique
options.description: Command each Bonded to spend reaction + 4 slots to ride your initial jutsu — each must cast a low-rank jutsu; if yours hits, theirs apply too. 10-min cooldown.

options.name: Tsunami (Ultimate)
options.description: On Water Release cast, affected creatures failing (or CON save if no save) increase jutsu cost by your level until end of your next turn.

features.name: Shinobi's Path
features.description:
A Scout-Nin's strength lies in flexibility, adaptation, and awareness. Choose one Shinobi Path (Awareness, Mender, Swift, Commandant, Archivist, Rogue). You gain an additional Path choice at 11th level.

PATH OF AWARENESS — Overly Perceptive (Search or Identify Genjutsu as a bonus action); Sensory Type (broaden perception to sense chakra on a Wisdom (Perception) check vs Chakra Control DC in 60 ft).

PATH OF THE MENDER — Field Medic (Administer Aid as a bonus action); Battle Medicine (Treat Wounds as an Action).

PATH OF THE SWIFT — Mastering the Basics (wall run / water walk without extra movement cost); Boosted Athleticism (+1 ft speed, +5 ft high/long jumps).

PATH OF THE COMMANDANT — Field Strategist (Help action as a bonus action even without proficiency); Tactical Callouts (once/turn reaction move half speed toward an ally and grant +2 AC/save if within 15 ft).

PATH OF THE ARCHIVIST — Encyclopedic Knowledge (Identify actions compare to target's Jutsu save DC without +5); Lore Extraction (Study as a Full-Turn Action; hyper-fixate Research for 1 hour, +1d8 bonus).

PATH OF THE ROGUE — Veiled Hand (Steal as a bonus action; one free lock-pick without a Security Kit); Shinobi Step (full movement when sneaking).

features.name: General Technique
features.description:
Choose one Fighting Stance (Taijutsu Stance, Weapon Stance, or Chakra Mode) from Chapter 13. You select an additional Stance of a type you have not chosen at 7th and 11th level. You can't take a Stance more than once.

When fighting with basic Shinobi implements (Kunai, Shuriken, Senbon), you can attack twice instead of once with the Attack action (but cannot bonus-attack with them that turn). You can also use Chili Pepper Bombs, Paper Bombs, Flash Tags, or Smoke Bombs in place of an attack made with your Attack action.

features.name: Adaptive Initiative
features.description:
When you roll Initiative, you may substitute your Initiative bonus with one of the following based on the encounter circumstances (GM may require a normal roll if the declared substitution isn't relevant):
  • Athletics — if you were doing something physically demanding.
  • Ninshou — if you could sense hostile chakra or were concentrating on a Ninjutsu.
  • Illusion — if you were investigating/concentrating on a Genjutsu or under one.
  • Insight — if you were aware of the target's hostile intent prior to combat.

features.name: Superior Technique
features.description: You gain a pool of 3 Superiority Dice (d8s) representing your refined technique. Gain more as you level per the Scout-Nin class table. Spend Superiority Dice when class features call for them. Spent dice recover on 10 minutes of uninterrupted rest or meditation.

features.name: Switch Hitter
features.description:
Select one casting discipline (Ninjutsu, Genjutsu, Taijutsu, or Bukijutsu) as your Primary. You can't select a Primary whose casting ability modifier is lower than another's.

When you cast your Primary jutsu, choose Follow Up or Set-Up:
  • Follow Up — one affected creature grants you a +1 to hit / save DC against them if the next jutsu cast against them isn't your Primary (+2 at 11th, +3 at 19th). Lasts until the end of your next turn.
  • Set-Up — all affected creatures suffer a -1 penalty to AC or save (your choice) against the next jutsu cast by one of your allies before your next turn (-2 at 11th, -3 at 19th).

features.name: Jack of All
features.description:
Select one Generalization: Warfare, Seize, or Tactics. Swap which you're benefiting from with a Bonus Action.

WARFARE — Add ability mod to Multiattack damage with Kunai/Shuriken/Senbon. Once/turn, spend 1 Superiority Die as part of casting a jutsu to add 1d8 damage (2d8 at 5, 3d8 at 11, 4d8 at 15, 5d8 at 20). Area jutsu roll half the listed dice (minimum 1). If used alongside a subclass Maneuver, reduce the bonus damage by 1 die.

SEIZE — While benefiting, when you force a creature to save from a Maneuver, item, or feature, spend 1 Bonus Action to deny them jutsu-granted save bonuses. Spend 1 Superiority Die on a contested check to add the result.

TACTICS — Skill-based actions requiring an Action become Bonus Actions; once/turn if a skill action costs a reaction, you use it at no action cost. Spend 1 Superiority Die as part of a skill action for +2 (+3 at 11, +4 at 20).

features.name: Scouting Technique
features.description: Choose a Scouting Technique (subclass). Gain features at 3rd, 6th, 10th, 14th, and 17th levels from it.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Increase one ability by +1, gain a Feat you qualify for, and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit. Repeats at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level. Cannot raise a score above 20.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description:
You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

With Kunai/Shuriken/Senbon, you can attack three times instead of twice. Chili Pepper Bombs, Paper Bombs, Flash Tags, and Smoke Bombs of base quality gain a d4 ammunition die instead of charges.

features.name: Superior Momentum
features.description: Once per turn, spend 2 Superiority Die to take one additional Action — only to Cast a Jutsu of a different casting discipline than a jutsu you cast on this turn. Using this feature again before a rest increases the Superiority Die cost by +1 per prior use.

features.name: Signature Jutsu
features.description:
Select one Jutsu you know of B-Rank or lower as your Signature Jutsu. It counts as your Primary regardless of discipline keyword when cast. Pick one Signature Modification (Power, Ramping, or Refinement). Swap the chosen jutsu on a Full Rest. Select a second Signature Modification at 15th level.

POWER — Ignores resistance, treats immunity as resistance. (Warfare) adds Jutsu ability mod to damage. (Seize) cast as a bonus action in place of an action.

RAMPING — Upcasting adds +1 damage/heal die. (Seize) reactions casting a jutsu against you increases that jutsu's cost by your level. (Tactics) fold a skill-based action into an attack-roll casting.

REFINEMENT — Reduces the cost by the jutsu's rank (D:1, C:2, B:3, A:4, S:5). (Tactics) +1d4 to check-based jutsu. (Warfare) use two Maneuvers per turn, both as part of the chosen jutsu's casting.

features.name: General Technique (2)
features.description: Select an additional Fighting Stance of a type you have not selected yet.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Second ASI/Feat. See level 4.

features.name: Signature Path
features.description:
Your originally chosen Shinobi Path gains an enhanced benefit (see Path descriptions). At 18th level, the enhanced benefit applies to your second Path as well.

PERCEPTION (Awareness) — attackers against you or allies within 10 ft have disadvantage; gain 20 ft Blindsight.

MEDIC (Mender) — Blood/Chakra Pills heal +5; Medicine Kit actions costing 2+ charges reduce cost by 1.

MOBILE (Swift) — You and allies within 10 ft of you can Dash/Disengage as bonus actions; ignore difficult terrain.

LEADER (Commandant) — Action battle-cry inspires allies within 60 ft: spend 2 HD & 2 CD to regain HP/CP = results + CON.

GENIUS (Archivist) — +2 to all proficient skill/toolkit checks; reroll natural 1s once.

HIDDEN TECHNIQUE (Rogue) — Action while lightly obscured makes you and allies within 5 ft Invisible to Search for 1 minute (or until movement/attack); undetectable by Tremor sense and Chakra sight.

features.name: General Technique (3)
features.description: Select an additional Fighting Stance of a type you have not selected yet.

features.name: Shinobi's Path (2)
features.description: Select a second Shinobi Path. Its Follow-Up/Set-Up scaling also upgrades to +2 this level.

features.name: Multifaceted
features.description:
When you cast a Jutsu as an Action, you can spend your Bonus Action to make 2 Unarmed or Weapon Attacks using a Kunai, Shuriken or Senbon.

When you take the Attack Action and make an Unarmed/Weapon attack with a Kunai, Shuriken, or Senbon, you can apply Follow Up to one creature damaged as part of your attacks.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Third ASI/Feat. See level 4.

features.name: Signature Switch
features.description:
When you deal damage with your Primary casting discipline, spend 1 Superiority Die to apply both Follow Up and Set-Up to the same hostile creature.

Once per casting, when you deal damage with a jutsu, you can switch your current Fighting Stance and Generalization after the damage calculation.

features.name: Signature Jutsu (2)
features.description: Select a second Signature Modification that your Signature Jutsu gains the benefits of.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Fourth ASI/Feat. See level 4.

features.name: Signature Path (2)
features.description: The enhanced benefit from Signature Path now also applies to your second Shinobi Path.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Fifth ASI/Feat. See level 4. Switch Hitter bonuses upgrade to +3 / -3.

features.name: Superior Scout
features.description:
You have become the pinnacle of adaptability and battlefield flexibility.
  • Regain 1 spent Superiority Die at the beginning of each of your turns.
  • All jutsu you cast of C-Rank and lower are treated as your Signature Jutsu.

subclasses.name: Arbiter Scout
subclasses.description: Focused on commanding and enabling allies — battlefield coordinators who turn a team into a force multiplier.

features.name: Superior Arbitration
features.description: Learn two Arbiter Maneuvers. Gain proficiency in Deception, Intimidation, and Persuasion; if already proficient in any, instead gain a rank of Mastery in that skill.

features.name: Battlefield Judication
features.description: On a rest, designate up to your proficiency bonus of other creatures as your Squad. In combat: Squad gains +CHA mod initiative. Once/turn in place of an attack from the Attack Action, Command a Squad-Mate who can see/hear you to: make a weapon attack, cast a D-Rank (or lower) attack-roll Jutsu (C-Rank at 10, B-Rank at 17), perform a skill-based action (regain 1 Superiority Die), Dodge, or Disengage-and-move half speed (regain 1 Superiority Die). When a Squad-Mate makes an attack, spend a Superiority Die + Reaction to attack the same target — on a hit, add the Superiority Die to both damage rolls.

features.name: Absolute Authority
features.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in one of Deception, Intimidation, or Persuasion. When yourself or a Squad-Mate performs a skill-based action using STR, DEX, or CHA, as a reaction you can substitute any CHA-based skill you have Mastery ranks in for the check.

features.name: Adaptive Orders
features.description: Squad-Mates no longer need to see/hear you to follow commands (must be in initiative with you). You may Command twice per turn instead of once, forgoing one of your attacks per command.

features.name: Commanding Presence
features.description:
Bonus Action, spend 2 Superiority Die: all Squad-Mates that can see/hear you in initiative that are under a hostile creature's effect immediately remake their saving throw, adding your Tactics Generalization skill bonus to the result.

Reaction on a Squad-Mate's turn, spend 3 Superiority Die: that Squad-Mate gains an additional Action per Superior Momentum rules (once/turn per creature).

features.name: Unbreakable Formation
features.description: You and Squad are immune to Confused while not incapacitated. Squad members beginning their turn within 10 ft of another Squad-Mate gain 10 temporary HP until the start of their next turn; while they have those temp HP, they gain advantage on saves vs forced movement/teleportation.

features.name: Who Decided That!?
features.description: When a Squad member other than you fails a save, spend 2 Superiority Die + Reaction to make them automatically succeed with no additional effects. Once per initiative per creature.

subclasses.name: Assault Scout
subclasses.description: Raw physical power honed to deadly perfection — brutal combatants who overwhelm enemies through aggression and focus.

features.name: Superior Assault
features.description: Learn two Assault Maneuvers. Gain a +1 bonus to your critical threat range with attacks made using your Primary casting discipline.

features.name: Brutish Momentum
features.description:
When initiative is rolled, mark a Combat Rival (visible creature; locked in until they or you reach 0 HP). While you have a Combat Rival:
  • Relentless Durability — once/turn, on damage taken spend 1 Superiority Die to gain temp HP = result (2× at 10th, 3× at 17th), lasting to end of turn.
  • Relentless Aggression — once/turn on a hit vs Rival, perform Trip/Grapple/Shove/Disarm using your attack result in place of the skill check.
  • Relentless Pursuit — when Rival moves 5+ ft away, reaction to move full speed closer and make an attack if in range, adding Superiority Die to damage.
  • Relentless Progress — first time/turn a hit on Rival exceeds AC by 5 (or they fail a save by 5), regain 1 Superiority Die.

features.name: Untapped Potential
features.description:
Once per turn you may use your Warfare bonus damage without spending a Superiority Die.

When a maneuver requires rolling a Superiority Die for its effect, you may roll it twice and take the higher result.

features.name: Brutish Aggression
features.description: When you succeed on a skill-based action targeting your Combat Rival, they have disadvantage on their next attack roll and you regain 1 Superiority Die (once/turn).

features.name: Remarkable Aggression
features.description:
Critical threat range with Primary casting discipline increases to +2. While you have a Combat Rival:
  • Relentless Stamina — once/turn, spend a Superiority Die to reduce ranks of Physical conditions gained by 1.
  • Relentless Focus — while concentrating on a jutsu, add your Tactics Generalization skill bonus to forced Chakra Control checks.

features.name: Devastating Assault
features.description: When you score a critical hit, spend 1 Superiority Die to maximize the damage (instead of rolling double dice), also doubling your ability modifier. If reused before a rest, increase cost by +1 per prior use.

features.name: Relentless
features.description: When using Superior Momentum, you can gain 2 Actions instead of 1 by increasing the Superiority Die cost by +2 (still requiring different casting disciplines).

subclasses.name: Cloning Scout
subclasses.description: Masters of duplicative combat — scouts who employ superior numbers via Clones and Bunshin to overwhelm enemies.

features.name: Superior Clones
features.description:
Learn two Cloning Maneuvers. Add Shadow Clone Technique to your known jutsu; you can cast it as a Bonus Action. Learn any Ninjutsu with the Clone keyword ignoring nature-release prereqs.

When you summon a clone, you may instead summon a Bunshin (see stat block). Up to 4 Bunshin active; while active, you can't have Clone-keyword summons. Bunshin last 1 min in combat / 1 hour out / up to 1 mile away; dispelled if they enter combat. On dispel you gain their information.

Bunshin commands:
  • Where I Say — once/turn, no action cost, move all active Bunshin to a space within movement.
  • Clone Strike — once/turn during Attack Action, in place of an attack command one or more Bunshin to make a Bunshin Strike or Cast.
  • Split Focus — once/turn as a Bonus Action, command a Bunshin to perform a skill action or Help an ally.

features.name: Tajū Kage Bunshin
features.description:
Spend 1 Superiority Die + Bonus Action to summon 1 Bunshin (2 at 10th, 3 at 17th). Your clones can use these class features following normal limits: Cloning Maneuvers, Switch Hitter, and Jack of All (your current Generalization).

When you summon 2+ Clones/Bunshin at once as part of casting a Ninjutsu with the Clone keyword, regain 1 Superiority Die.

features.name: Clones Everywhere
features.description:
Once per turn, while you have 1+ Clone/Bunshin active, when you, a clone, or a Bunshin scores a hit, the damaged creature makes a Wisdom save vs your Stealth DC or becomes Disoriented.

Clones help you learn/modify/create jutsu. Reduce downtime for those tasks by the rank of the jutsu (D:1, C:2, B:3, A:4, S:5).

features.name: Never Alone
features.description:
When you attack or cast a jutsu, command active Bunshin to assist (they cannot attack or cast that turn; they move as close to you as possible ignoring speed).
  • Army of One — +2 damage per Bunshin assisting (max +10).
  • Pivot! — once/turn, command a Bunshin to Strike or Cast.
  • Bunshin Throw — 2+ assist: teleport to a space ≤ 10 ft × number of assistants; regain 1 Superiority Die.
  • Bunshin Amp — 3+ assist: dispel up to 4 to upcast an upcastable jutsu at no extra cost per 2 Bunshin dispelled; regain 1 Superiority Die.

features.name: Specialized Clones
features.description:
Max Bunshin increases to 6. When a Clone-keyword jutsu has a nature release keyword, summoned Bunshin gain an element bonus (max 2 different keywords active at once):
  • Earth — HP 10 instead of 1; 10 DR and 10 temp HP; 10 temp HP regen at start of each turn.
  • Fire — on drop to 0 HP, all creatures within 10 ft make a Dexterity save vs Ninjutsu DC or take 6d6 fire damage (2 saves/turn max).
  • Water — take no damage on passed saves; use your Ninjutsu attack bonus in place of save bonus.
  • Lightning — Bunshin damage uses d8s instead of d6s.

features.name: Clone Tactics
features.description: When you take damage or fail a save, spend your Reaction to summon a Bunshin in your space then teleport to another Bunshin within 60 ft (dispelling that one). If the landing is lightly obscured, you can attempt Hide.

features.name: Clone Master
features.description: Max Bunshin increases to 8. Clone-keyword jutsu summoning extra Bunshin cost 2 chakra per Bunshin beyond the first. Clone Strike can be used as many times/turn as attacks in your Attack Action.

resources.name: Bunshin (active)
resources.maxFormulaDescription: 4 at L3, 6 at L10, 8 at L17

subclasses.name: Elemental Scout
subclasses.description: Nature-release savants who let their chosen element flow constantly through their chakra, amplifying jutsu and their own body alike.

features.name: Superior Elements
features.description:
Learn two Elemental Maneuvers. Select one nature release (Earth / Wind / Fire / Water / Lightning), gaining access to learn/cast jutsu with that keyword.

Elemental Infusion (Strike) — after casting a nature-release jutsu, until end of next turn your unarmed and weapon attacks deal the element's damage type.

Once per turn you can add your chosen nature-release keyword to a jutsu that doesn't already have one.

features.name: Elemental Flux
features.description:
When you cast a nature-release jutsu in combat, you may enter Elemental Flux until the end of your next turn. Casting another jutsu with the same nature release extends duration by 1 round; casting a different nature release switches Flux type and resets duration.

Once per turn, while in Flux, when you cast a jutsu that applies or benefits from Follow Up or Set-Up, regain 1 Superiority Die.

Each Flux type grants bonuses per discipline (Taijutsu/Bukijutsu temp HP or +crit; Ninjutsu terrain/turbulence/DR pierce; Genjutsu slow/distortion/etc.). See PDF for full table.

features.name: Perceived Weakness
features.description:
In place of an attack during Attack Action or as a Bonus Action, make a Maintain Focus check vs a target's CON Save DC (60 ft). On success:
  • Natural Perception — you become aware of the target's accessible nature releases.
  • Weakness Tracing — target gains Momentary Flaw condition (exploitable only by your nature-release techniques) until end of your next turn.

features.name: Elemental Aura
features.description:
Select a second nature release. You radiate a 10-ft aura — creatures of your choice in the aura gain a save bonus vs nature-release jutsu = half your CON mod.

While in Elemental Flux, gain resistance to damage of your current Flux type.

features.name: Resonance
features.description: When an ally in your Elemental Aura casts a nature-release jutsu, spend 1 Superiority Die to enter Flux of that nature release. The triggering ally adds 1d8 damage or reduces chakra cost by half the result (their choice).

features.name: Primordial Flux
features.description: Elemental Aura increases to 20 ft. Once/turn when you enter Flux, allies in the aura gain 1d8 temporary chakra. Elemental Infusion adds 1d8 bonus damage to unarmed/weapon attacks as part of Attack Action or Bonus Action.

features.name: Primal Storm
features.description: At the start of your turn while in Flux, spend 1 Superiority Die to gain immunity to your current Flux damage type until the start of your next turn. While in Flux, use one additional Maneuver per turn.

natureReleaseGrants.description: Select one nature release (Earth, Wind, Fire, Water, or Lightning) to gain the ability to learn and cast jutsu with that keyword.
Select a second nature release (from the five elements). Your picker excludes any release you already possess.

subclasses.name: Phantom Scout
subclasses.description: Ghostly infiltrators and assassins — masters of stealth, deception, and illusory combat who fade in and out of sight.

features.name: Superior Phantasm
features.description:
Learn two Phantom Maneuvers. Gain proficiency in Deception and Illusions; if already proficient in either, instead gain a rank of Mastery in that skill.

Ghastly Deception — in place of an attack during Attack Action or as a Bonus Action, make a Deception check vs target's AC (20 ft). On success, teleport into a space within 10 ft of target; applies Follow-Up from Switch Hitter.

features.name: Phantom's Flame
features.description:
You build and spend Ghostfire to fuel phantasmic powers.

Ghostfire — once/turn when you benefit from Follow Up or perform a skill-based action, gain 1 Ghostfire (max 3).

Phantasm's Grip — on an attack / attack-roll jutsu, spend 1 Ghostfire to make it count as Genjutsu (psychic or chakra damage); conditions become Concussed. If the attack is part of the Attack Action, target also saves WIS vs Ninjutsu/Genjutsu DC or takes 1 rank of Concussed. Attack result 5+ over AC regains 1 Superiority Die.

Ghost Walk — Bonus as part of movement, spend 1 Ghostfire to become incorporeal until end of turn (can't pass jutsu-made or chakra-reinforced matter). Shunted out of solid matter on turn end costs 10 necrotic/5 ft (unreducible).

Gravesight — Bonus Action, 1 Ghostfire: 30 ft Blindsight, see Invisible, discern Clones/constructs/undead until end of next turn.

features.name: Haunting Veil
features.description: While not in combat and attempting Hide, all allies within 10 ft gain Spectral Concealment (uses your Stealth result, undetectable to Chakra sight/Darkvision, 1 min or until observed). Regain 1 Superiority Die (once/10 minutes).

features.name: Hungry Phantom Flame
features.description: Reaction on damage taken: spend 1 Superiority Die + any Ghostfire to reduce damage by 2d6 per Ghostfire (2d8 if triggering attack is Genjutsu or Psychic damage). If reduced to 0, all hostile creatures within 10 ft save WIS vs Ninjutsu/Genjutsu DC or take chakra damage equal to the reduction. Gain 1d6 temporary chakra per Ghostfire spent (1 min).

features.name: Phantasm's Inferno
features.description:
Max Ghostfire increases to 5. When an ally benefits from Set-Up, gain 1 Ghostfire.

Spectral Brand — after dealing psychic/chakra damage, spend 1 Superiority Die + 1 Ghostfire to brand (1 min). Branded creatures are always Set-Up affected (benefit once/turn). Detonate as an Action: branded creatures save WIS vs Ninjutsu/Genjutsu DC or gain 2 ranks Sealed + 2 ranks Concussed.

Hollow Reflection — on a Mental/Sensory condition from a visible caster, spend Reaction + 1 Superiority Die + 1 Ghostfire; target saves INT vs your DC or your ranks of Mental/Sensory conditions drop to 0 and they gain 2 ranks of each.

features.name: Between the Veil
features.description:
When you place yourself + at least one other creature under Spectral Concealment, pick one enhancement (locked for 10 minutes):
  • Dimensional Walk — while Sneaking, all concealed creatures gain Ghost Walk at no Ghostfire cost.
  • Extended Veil — Spectral Concealment lasts 10 minutes; can cast non-harmful jutsu without breaking stealth.

features.name: Phantasmic Eruption
features.description: While in Ghost Walk, on a melee hit spend 3 Ghostfire: target saves CHA vs your Ninjutsu/Genjutsu DC or becomes Ghostburned (chakra network burned and overloaded).

subclasses.name: Tactical Scout
subclasses.description: Battlefield academics — tactical specialists who turn combat into an exercise in planning, preparation, and overwhelming knowledge.

features.name: Superior Tactics
features.description:
Learn two Tactical Maneuvers. Select one additional Shinobi Path from the Shinobi's Path class feature.

Once per turn when an ally attacks or casts a jutsu, spend a Superiority Die to share one of your known Maneuvers with them (if applicable).

features.name: Master of Tactics
features.description:
When you roll initiative, pick any Adaptive Initiative skill regardless of circumstance and roll that (or normal initiative) at advantage.

Gain 1 Tactical Insight at the start of your turn or once/turn when you perform a skill-based action (max 4):
