  • Always Prepared — spend Tactical Insight in place of Superiority Die on non-maneuver features.
  • Updated Tactics — spend 1 to switch Jack of All Generalization at no action cost.
  • Follow Through — spend 1 instead of applying just Follow Up or Set-Up, apply both.
  • Tactical Placement — spend 1 to reroll initiative; your position in initiative moves to the new result (no double-turns).

features.name: Better Than the Best
features.description: Select any two Maneuvers from other Scout-Nin subclasses (Arbiter, Assault, Cloning, Elemental, Phantom). These don't count against your Maneuvers Known. You cannot pick maneuvers with the Specialized keyword from another subclass. Select +2 more at 14th level.

features.name: Role Reassignment
features.description:
On a rest, pick one role until your next rest:
  • Infiltrator — move through occupied enemy spaces (same size or larger); undetectable by traps/alarms while moving.
  • Field Analyst — Bonus Action to study a visible creature (Insight or Investigation vs CHA Save DC); learn their highest physical score and lowest mental score.
  • Tactical Swap — Bonus Action, spend 1 Superiority Die to swap places (and initiative position) with a willing ally.

features.name: Exceeding Expectations
features.description:
Use one additional Maneuver per turn. Gain a unique Generalization called Tactician (spending Tactical Insight for effects):
  • Lasting Affliction — 2 TI: inflicted Physical/Mental/Sensory conditions gain [Persists] for 1 min; require CON save vs your Jutsu DC to end.
  • Always Prepared — once/turn, 2 TI: inflict a specific chosen Physical/Mental/Sensory condition (excl. Staggered, Restrained, Lacerated, Berserk, Charmed, Blinded) in place of a jutsu's inflicted condition.
  • Never Caught Off Guard — 2 TI: Superior Momentum grants an additional reaction usable until your next turn.

features.name: Mastered Path
features.description: Select a third Shinobi Path and gain Signature Path benefits for the Path you selected with Superior Tactics.

features.name: The Elite
features.description: Reduce the Superiority Die cost of Superior Momentum by 1. When you cast two different jutsu with different casting disciplines on the same turn (or Attack + Primary cast), regain 1 Superiority Die. Use two additional Maneuvers per turn instead of one.

pickable.editorDescription: Scout-Nin maneuvers, learned via the level-up window. Non-specialized maneuvers are available to any Scout-Nin; specialized ones require the matching Scouting Technique. Use + Custom for homebrew or Tactical's Better-Than-The-Best picks.
pickable.pickerDescription: Scout-Nin Maneuvers scale with level (2 at 3, +1 each at 7/11/15/19). Maneuvers marked as Specialized require your specific Scouting Technique.

options.name: Back Off!
options.description: Bonus Action, 1 Superiority Die: all Squad members move up to half speed away from hostiles without provoking; gain DR equal to the Superiority Die result until the start of your next turn.

options.name: Pincer Attack
options.description: Bonus Action, 1 Superiority Die when 2+ Squad members engage the same creature: each engaged Squad-Mate makes an Unarmed/Weapon attack against that creature, adding the Superiority Die to damage. Each successful strike imposes -1 on the target's next save vs a jutsu you cast before end of turn.

options.name: Guards Up!
options.description: Reaction when a visible creature within 30 ft would take damage from a failed save or an attack: spend 1 Superiority Die. They gain bonus to AC or save vs the triggering effect — AC: full result; Save: half result.

options.name: Go All Out
options.description: Bonus Action, 1 Superiority Die: select a Squad-Mate and command them to spend their reaction to cast a B-Rank or lower 1-Action jutsu. If cast, add your Warfare bonus to damage; area effects roll half dice (min 1).

options.name: I Know What I'm Doing
options.description: When you attempt a skill-based action, spend a Superiority Die to remove its action cost. Once per turn if in combat.

options.name: Follow My Lead
options.description: Melee attack + 1 Superiority Die: gain your Follow-Up bonus to hit. On hit, apply Set-Up, then select a Squad-Mate to spend their reaction to make the same attack type (or cast same-rank jutsu) against the same target.

options.name: Authoritative Strike
options.description: On hit or on a jutsu save failed: spend 1 Superiority Die. All affected hostile creatures gain 1 rank of Demoralized or Weakened until the start of your next turn.

options.name: Leader's Charge
options.description: Spend 2 Superiority Die as movement begins: +20 ft speed. On a hit at end of movement against an adjacent creature, all Squad-Mates can spend reactions to move full speed and attack/cast C-Rank or lower against the same target, adding the Superiority Die to their damage.

options.name: Shared Willpower
options.description: On hit / jutsu save failed: spend 1 Superiority Die. If you are under a jutsu-based effect requiring repeat saves, a Squad-Mate may make a save using their stats in place of yours; on success the effect ends on you.

options.name: Leader's Arbitration
options.description: Action, 1 Superiority Die: creatures of your choice within 30 ft save WIS or INT (your choice) vs your Ninjutsu/Genjutsu DC. On fail, for 1 minute they can't Disengage or lie to you or your allies.

options.name: Amplified Force
options.description: When casting a jutsu forcing your Combat Rival to save vs a condition, spend 1 Superiority Die. On fail, they gain +1 rank of that condition.

options.name: Arresting Strike
options.description: On hit: spend 1 Superiority Die. Target saves STR vs your attack DC (Taijutsu if weapon/unarmed). On fail, speed halved, knocked prone, and can't stand without using Bonus Action until end of their next turn.

options.name: Rending Critical
options.description: On a critical hit vs your Combat Rival: spend 1 Superiority Die. Reduce target's AC and DR by half the Superiority Die result. Rival can't gain jutsu-based bonuses to AC/DR until your next turn.

options.name: Knock Back
options.description: On hit: spend 1 Superiority Die. Push target 10 × result feet away, ending prone. If stopped by a structure ≤5 ft thick, destroy it and continue; target takes 2d6 bludgeoning per structure.

options.name: Whirlwind Strike
options.description: On damage to your Combat Rival with an attack: spend 1 Superiority Die. Select up to (result) creatures within 10 ft of target; compare your attack to their AC. Each hit takes half the original damage.

options.name: Menacing Assault
options.description: On damage to your Combat Rival: spend 1 Superiority Die. Creatures of your choice within 20 ft of you or the target save WIS vs your Attack/Jutsu DC or gain ranks of Demoralized = half Superiority Die result.

options.name: Just You
options.description: On an area jutsu that includes your Combat Rival: spend 1 Superiority Die. The jutsu loses its area and only affects your Rival; they make their save at disadvantage.

options.name: Limit Breaker
options.description: No action cost, spend 1 Superiority Die: until your next turn, can't be reduced below 1 HP by attacks/jutsu; add Superiority Die to first attack each turn; first incoming attack each turn has advantage. Reuse before rest increases cost by +1 each time.

options.name: Relentless Retaliation
options.description: On damage taken by a creature in range: spend 2 Superiority Dice + Reaction. Make an attack or cast a known jutsu at the triggering creature. On hit or failed save, repeat at -4/+4 each subsequent time (cumulative by ±2).

options.name: Staggering Will
options.description: On a failed save that deals damage and inflicts a condition: spend 1 Superiority Die, add result to the save. If this flips to success, hostile creatures within 20 ft save WIS vs your Jutsu DC or Staggered until your next turn.

options.name: Clones Stealth
options.description: When you summon 2+ Bunshin and are within 60 ft of obscuring terrain that can hold them: spend 1 Superiority Die, make a Stealth check vs highest passive Perception, adding Superiority Die result. On success, Bunshin are undetected.

options.name: Clone Swap
options.description: Reaction on damage to yourself or an ally: spend a Superiority Die. A clone/Bunshin within 15 ft swaps places with the target and takes the damage and effects instead.

options.name: Clone Combo
options.description: On attack with at least one Bunshin active: spend 1 Superiority Die. Dismiss any number of Bunshin — each -1 to target AC (max -3) and -2 DR (max -6) for the triggering attack.

options.name: Clone Flank
options.description: When you summon 2+ clones/Bunshin with a hostile within 60 ft: spend 1 Superiority Die. For each Bunshin adjacent to target, -1 to their next STR/DEX/CON save (max -3) until end of your next turn.

options.name: Clone Raid
options.description: When a hostile within 10 ft of a clone/Bunshin casts a jutsu granting HP or temp HP: Reaction + 1 Superiority Die. Roll 1d8 per clone within 10 ft of them; reduce HP/temp HP gained by result.

options.name: Clone Swarm
options.description: When casting an attack-roll jutsu: each miss may be remade by spending 1 Superiority Die + dismissing a Bunshin. Remade attacks lose advantage.

options.name: Durable Clone
options.description: On damage taken by you or a clone/Bunshin you control: spend a Superiority Die. The affected creature gains DR equal to the result.

options.name: Elite Clone
options.description: When summoning 2+ Bunshin: spend 1 Superiority Die during casting. Summon only one Bunshin instead, with HP = 5 × result; +1 jutsu cast; +1d8 damage once/turn. Max one Elite Bunshin active.

options.name: Cloned Technique
options.description: Select a Bunshin, spend 1 Superiority Die: until end of turn, that Bunshin deals the jutsu's listed damage on hit.

options.name: Clones Support
options.description: Reaction on a save: spend 1 Superiority Die. Dismiss any number of clones/Bunshin; +1 save per dismissed (max +4).

options.name: Elemental Disruption
options.description: On damage to a creature from an attack while in Elemental Flux: spend 1 Superiority Die. Until start of your next turn, the target's jutsu cost increases by twice the Superiority Die result.

options.name: Elemental Being
options.description: Reaction on damage taken by an attack while in Flux: spend 1 Superiority Die. +AC = result until end of turn. If triggering attacks are melee, deal damage (type = Flux) to attackers until end of turn, equal to twice the Superiority Die result.

options.name: Elemental Malady
options.description: When casting a nature-release jutsu that can inflict an Elemental condition: spend 1 Superiority Die. It inflicts additional ranks of that condition equal to half the Superiority Die result (on fail/hit as usual).

options.name: Heightened Jutsu
options.description: When casting a nature-release jutsu: spend 1 Superiority Die. The jutsu upcasts by +1 rank at no extra cost, ignoring normal limits.

options.name: Natured Jutsu
options.description: When an ally within 30 ft casts a jutsu while you're in Flux: spend 1 Superiority Die. Their jutsu gains your Flux's nature-release keyword and benefits from your Flux's shared bonuses.

options.name: Elemental Chakra-Strike
options.description: Bonus Action, 1 Superiority Die: select a known Ninjutsu/Genjutsu requiring an attack roll. It gains your nature release and is infused until end of your next turn. Next hit deals Weapon/Unarmed damage + jutsu damage; if the jutsu requires a save, target saves at disadvantage.

options.name: Primal Recovery
options.description: Bonus Action while in Flux: spend 1 Superiority Die. Gain temp chakra = 2 × result; regain ability to mold chakra if lost. Flux ends. Requires 10 min to recharge; reuse before recharge +1 cost each time.

options.name: Primal Power
options.description: Spend 2 Superiority Dice. Your next attack is made at disadvantage and can't gain advantage or jutsu-based bonuses. On a hit, treat as a critical hit.

options.name: Primal Enhancement
options.description: When casting a jutsu that reduces damage or grants temp HP while in Flux: spend 1 Superiority Die. Increase the effect by the Superiority Die result.

options.name: Shared Flux
options.description: Action, 1 Superiority Die: transfer Flux to a creature within 30 ft. Your Flux ends; their Flux lasts until your next turn. You can still spend Superiority Dice to activate maneuvers on their behalf during that time.

options.name: Spirit Siphon
options.description: On chakra damage to a creature: spend 1 Superiority Die. Target saves WIS vs attack DC (Taijutsu if weapon/unarmed) or gains 1 rank of Slow. If already suffering a Mental condition, the save is at disadvantage.

options.name: Poltergeist Visage
options.description: In place of an attack from Attack Action: spend 1 Superiority Die. Hostiles within 20 ft save WIS vs your Ninjutsu/Genjutsu DC or drop weapons, move full speed away; can't cast hand-sign jutsu until their next turn.

options.name: Ghastly Wail
options.description: In place of an attack from Attack Action: spend 1 Superiority Die. 30-ft cone: creatures save CHA vs your Ninjutsu/Genjutsu DC or gain 2 ranks of Dazzled.

options.name: Phantom Avoidance
options.description: Reaction when hit by an attack or failing a damage save: spend 1 Superiority Die. Gain DR = 5 × your current Ghostfire count.

options.name: Ghostfire Claw
options.description: When using Phantom's Grip: spend 1 Superiority Die. If the target would gain Concussed, they also gain 1 rank of Sealed. From Hidden/Unaware, the save is at disadvantage, and on fail: +1 Concussed + 2 Sealed.

options.name: Haunting
options.description: When a creature within 5 ft moves away: Reaction + 1 Superiority Die. Become invisible, move up to speed ignoring terrain/other creatures' spaces, end as close as possible to triggering creature, invisibility ends, make one weapon attack adding Superiority Die to damage.

options.name: Geist Walk
options.description: When making a save or skill check to pass through a barrier (e.g., Adamantine Barrier, Forcecage): spend 1 Superiority Die, add result to check.

options.name: Shadow Snatch
options.description: When a visible creature within 30 ft would hit an ally with an attack: Reaction + 1 Superiority Die. Reduce the attack roll by the Superiority Die result.

options.name: Shadow Drain
options.description: When a visible creature within 30 ft gains a bonus to hit or damage: Reaction + 1 Superiority Die. They save WIS vs your Ninjutsu/Genjutsu DC or lose the bonus, and you gain it until your next turn.

options.name: Superior Disarming Trip
options.description: On a hit: spend 1 Superiority Die. Infuse Disarm, Trip, and Shove into the attack; use your attack roll result in place of each skill check, compared to target's AC.

options.name: Superior Feint
options.description: On a hit: spend 1 Superiority Die. Infuse Demoralize and Feint into the attack; use your attack roll result in place of each skill check, compared to target's WIS Save DC.

options.name: All A Part Of The Plan
options.description: Bonus Action, spend 1 Superiority Die: roll 1d20 and record the result (max 3 stored for 1 min). On a save, substitute your roll with any recorded result.

options.name: Superior Riposte
options.description: When a creature misses you with an attack: Reaction + 1 Superiority Die. Make a melee/ranged weapon attack; on hit, target gains 1 rank of Weakened or Demoralized.

options.name: Superior Suppression
options.description: When a creature under your cast jutsu's effect saves to resist: spend 1 Superiority Die. Reroll the save, taking the lower result. On fail, they gain 1 rank of Demoralized or Sealed.

options.name: Tactical Breakthrough
options.description: On damage: spend 1 Superiority Die. If damage would be reduced by DR or temp HP, target saves CON vs attack/jutsu DC (Taijutsu if weapon/unarmed). On fail, reduce their DR or temp HP by 2 × Superiority Die result until end of turn.

options.name: Tactical Escape
options.description: Reaction on a hostile's Action: spend 1 Superiority Die. Gain Disengage + Dash, move full speed. If you make a STR (Athletics) or DEX (Acrobatics) check before end of turn, add Superiority Die to result.

options.name: Tactical Overwrite
options.description: On an attack targeting a creature: spend 1 Superiority Die. If target would gain reaction-based bonuses to AC, they save Chakra Control vs your attack save DC (Taijutsu if weapon/unarmed) or lose the bonus until end of turn.

options.name: Tactical Parry
options.description: When taking damage and using your reaction to cast a damage-reducing jutsu: spend 1 Superiority Die. Add twice the result to that jutsu's reduction.

options.name: Tactical Strike
options.description: During Attack Action: forgo one attack + spend 1 Superiority Die to mix in a jutsu casting. Cast a C-Rank or lower jutsu that requires at most one attack; then finish your remaining attacks. Reuse on same turn increases cost by +1 per prior use (resets at end of turn).

masteryGrants.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.

features.name: Martial Technique
features.description:
While wearing no armor, your AC equals 10 + Proficiency + Dexterity Modifier. Choose one Taijutsu stance from Chapter 13, and while in a stance (or wielding a proficient weapon) you gain:
  • Martial Strength — unarmed damage die is d6 (→ d8 at 7th, d10 at 13th, d12 at 19th). The Unarmed Strike attack on your character sheet updates automatically with your level.
  • Agile Combat — use STR or DEX for unarmed/Taijutsu attack & save-DC calcs. Bonus-action unarmed attack available. Your sheet gets a second 'Unarmed Strike (Agile)' attack using DEX.
  • Unwasted Movements — your Taijutsu cost becomes Special per rank: D=3, C=5, B=9, A=14, S=20.

features.name: Enhanced Movement
features.description: Dash as a bonus action; speed +10 (→ +15 at 5 with doubled jump distance on Dash, +20 at 11, +25 at 15, +30 at 20).

features.name: Martial Adept
features.description: Your Martial Die start as d6 and grow with your Martial Strength damage die (d8/d10/d12 at 7/13/19). At the start of each turn you manifest a pool of Martial Die per the class table; spend them on Martial Techniques and class features; unspent dice are lost at the start of your next turn. You begin knowing two Martial Techniques; learn more per the class table. Techniques use your Taijutsu save DC.

features.name: Chakra Valves
features.description:
The first time each turn you deal damage from the Attack action or a Taijutsu, spend one Martial Die for one of:
  • Sustained Pressure — add that Martial Die to the triggering unarmed attack, and every subsequent unarmed attack this turn.
  • Valve Break — on a damaging Taijutsu, add Martial Die to damage equal to its rank (D:1, C:2, B:3, A:4, S:5).

features.name: Physical Genius
features.description: Learn and create Taijutsu in half the time and without lifestyle Ryo. Reduce the final chakra cost of newly created Taijutsu by its rank (D:1, C:2, B:3, A:4, S:5). You have advantage on saves/checks to resist ranks of Exhaustion.

features.name: Student of Perfection
features.description:
Select a Path of Perfection (Tyrant, Predator, Technician, Savage, Hero, or Genius) — paraphrased summary:
  • Tyrant — Taijutsu gain the Brutal keyword (+2 dmg, +4 at 11); Stolen Hope triggers attack-of-opportunity on adjacent HS jutsu; Victimizer (9th) prevents advantage on saves vs brutal-inflicted physical conditions.
  • Predator — Furious keyword (-2 DR per damage instance); Can't Run (-5 ft speed on unarmed hit); Never Escaping (9th) versus 0-speed targets.
  • Technician — Technical keyword (concentrate via Martial Arts); Unbreakable Focus; Perfectionists Musing (9th) can't be reacted/dispelled.
  • Savage — Devastating keyword (+1 crit); Unstoppable Force reaction-cast; Fueled By Ruin (9th) refund half chakra on crit.
  • Hero — Protective keyword (+1 AC to nearby allies, max +2); Guardian Force shares reaction-cast with allies; Champion (9th) grants 10 temp HP per damage instance (max 20).
  • Genius — Elite keyword (+1 attack); The New Era (+1 save DC); Next Generation (9th) concentration-check replace with Athletics.

features.name: Taijutsu Style
features.description: Choose a Taijutsu Style (subclass). Features unlock at levels 3, 6, 10, 14, 17.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Increase one ability by +1, gain a Feat you qualify for, and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit. Repeats at 8, 12, 16, 19. Cap 20.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. At 11th level, when you take the Attack action, you may use a bonus action to make 2 unarmed attacks.

features.name: Moment of Stillness
features.description:
Select one of three techniques (cannot be changed later): Bone Breaker, Balance Breaker, or Flow Breaker. On a successful unarmed or Taijutsu attack that deals unarmed damage, spend 2 Martial Dice to force the relevant save; fail = inflicts the Broken condition. Reuse blocked until end of next turn.

Select a second at 11th level.

features.name: Martial Strength (upgrade)
features.description: Unarmed damage die increases to d8. The sheet's Unarmed Strike updates automatically.

features.name: Iron Body
features.description: Gain a rank of Mastery with Constitution saves (→ 2 ranks at 13). You gain 2 Guard Slots of minor armor seals while unarmored on Full Rest (+1 at 9, replace one with a greater/refined seal at 13).

features.name: Agile
features.description: Initiative bonus = highest of INT/WIS/CHA mod. Successful DEX saves for half take no damage (full on fail). Disengage as a bonus action (+ Dodge at 13).

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Second ASI/Feat. See level 4.

features.name: Iron Will
features.description: Gain a rank of Mastery with Wisdom saves (→ 2 ranks at 15). Once per turn, add 1 Martial Die to a save vs a Fuinjutsu-keyword jutsu.

features.name: Expert of the Fist
features.description: You can use up to two Martial Techniques per trigger. Become an Expert in one known Taijutsu stance (select a Taijutsu Stance Expertise from Chapter 13).

features.name: Extra Attack (upgrade)
features.description: When you take the Attack action, as a bonus action you may make 2 unarmed attacks.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Third ASI/Feat. See level 4.

features.name: Martial Strength (upgrade)
features.description: Unarmed damage die increases to d10. The sheet's Unarmed Strike updates automatically.

features.name: Persistence
features.description: When saving against a Physical or Sensory condition, spend 1 Martial Die to add to the result. If you start your turn with a Physical or Sensory condition, spend 3 Martial Die to end one.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Fourth ASI/Feat. See level 4.

features.name: Martial Strength (upgrade)
features.description: Unarmed damage die increases to d12. The sheet's Unarmed Strike updates automatically.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Fifth ASI/Feat. See level 4.

features.name: Mastery of Self
features.description: Use each Chakra Valve ability at no Martial Die cost. Immunity to Envenomed, Exhaustion, Dazed. CON/WIS saves for half take no damage on success (half on fail). Gain a rank of Mastery in CON & WIS saves.

subclasses.name: Disturbance
subclasses.description: Trickster brawlers who overwhelm with multi-angle flurries and combo-breaking strikes — capable of halting even the most focused opponents mid-cast.

features.name: Disturbance Flurry
features.description:
Once per turn, when you deal unarmed damage, gain 1 Momentum (max 4, 1 min). Spend on:
  • Blitzing Momentum — +10 ft speed this turn; reactions granting movement gain +10 ft.
  • Surging Momentum — once/turn, 2 Momentum: gain an additional bonus action, only for a D-rank Taijutsu (→ C at 10, B at 17).
  • Disturbing Momentum — once/turn on hostile's turn, 2 Momentum: extra reaction to cast Taijutsu or use a Martial Technique.

features.name: Blinding Speed
features.description:
Learn these Martial Techniques:
  • Shatter — reaction to Ninjutsu/Genjutsu casting: spend 1 Martial Die, move half speed toward caster, make 2 unarmed attacks; on ≥1 hit, target Chakra Control check vs your Taijutsu DC — fail Interrupts the casting.
  • Unstable Drive — in place of an AoO attack, spend 1 Martial Die to cast a D-rank Taijutsu (→ C at 10, B at 17) targeting the trigger.

features.name: Unpredictable
features.description: Creatures can't target you with attacks of opportunity or reaction-attacks. Creatures casting Ninjutsu/Genjutsu while adjacent to you trigger an attack of opportunity from you.

features.name: Conversational Feint
features.description: Reaction to a creature passing a CHA check: make a Martial Arts or Deception check vs their WIS save — success grants a lie plus nullifies their benefit.

features.name: Unmatched Reflexes
features.description:
Max Momentum → 6. New spends:
  • Overwhelming Momentum — once/turn on a save vs your Taijutsu: 1 Momentum + 1 Martial Die, target can't react unless they take 2 ranks of Dazzled.
  • Imperceivable Momentum — on Ninjutsu/Taijutsu cast by a hostile in reach: reaction + 1 Momentum + 1 Martial Die to teleport within 5 ft and use another reaction-cost class feature.
  • Debilitating Momentum — on 2+ attacks vs the same creature in an action: 2 Momentum + 2 Martial Die, CON save vs Taijutsu DC — fail = Vulnerable to next damage (by any other creature).

features.name: Close the Distance
features.description: On ranged damage, spend any Momentum to reduce damage by 1d10 and move 10 ft toward the source per Momentum spent.

features.name: Disturbing Technique
features.description: When casting a Taijutsu that requires 2+ attacks and 2+ hostiles are in range, spend 3 Martial Die to target ALL hostile creatures in range, applying the jutsu's effects against each.

subclasses.name: Nin-Tai
subclasses.description: Elemental-infused martial artists — they coat their body in nature-release chakra and blend Taijutsu with Ninjutsu finishers.

features.name: Nature Enhanced Combat
features.description:
Focus your innate element. If you don't have a nature release, select one (Earth, Wind, Fire, Water, or Lightning Release). Your Taijutsu gain your chosen nature-release keyword if they don't already have one.

Bonus action + 1 Martial Die: enter Elemental Cloak state for 1 minute (or until you end early). Once ended, requires 10 minutes of meditation to re-enter.

ELEMENTAL CLOAK (Fortitude Amp)
Taijutsu you cast deals its nature release's associated damage type, and your chosen nature release grants your cloak different benefits:
  • Earth Release. You gain +4 DR. This DR cannot be reduced by any means. This becomes +6 at 10th and +8 at 17th level.
  • Wind Release. You gain a +1 bonus to AC, and damage you deal with Taijutsu penetrates 2 of a creature's DR. When you reach 10th level, you gain immunity to the Grappled and Restrained conditions. Finally, when you reach 17th level, your bonuses become +2 to AC and penetrate 4 DR.
  • Fire Release. You gain a rank of Mastery to [Unarmed Damage]. This becomes two ranks of Mastery at 10th and three ranks at 17th level.
  • Water Release. Your unarmed attacks gain the Reach 1 and Trip weapon properties. Beginning at 10th level, the first time each turn you deal [Unarmed Damage] to a creature you can move them up to 10 feet in any direction of your choice. Finally, at 17th level, creatures cannot gain jutsu-based bonuses to their saving throws vs Taijutsu you cast.
  • Lightning Release. Your unarmed and Taijutsu attacks gain a +1 bonus to their critical threat range and a +10 to speed. Beginning at 10th level, when you score a critical with an unarmed attack, you may add 1 unarmed damage die to the result. Finally, at 17th level, [Unarmed Damage] you deal cannot have its damage intercepted, always affecting its original target.

features.name: Burst Techniques
features.description:
Learn these Martial Techniques:
  • Elemental Rush — on a melee Taijutsu hit: 1 Martial Die, cast a D-rank Ninjutsu of the same nature release (1 Action cast time) as a bonus action using Taijutsu ability mod (→ C at 10, B at 17).
  • Elemental Crush — on a Finisher Taijutsu that would use its finisher effects: 1 Martial Die to apply your Elemental Finisher instead. Once/turn.

ELEMENTAL FINISHER
Taijutsu you cast with the Finisher keyword that are changed to become an Elemental Finisher gain the effects of the corresponding nature release:
  • Earth Release. You strike the ground creating a massive tectonic shift. All creatures in a 20-foot radius, centering on a point of the space you are occupying must make a Strength or Dexterity saving throw (your choice). On a failed save, they take your [Unarmed Damage] + earth damage equal to the rank of the Jutsu cast, are knocked prone, and you create an area of difficult terrain equal to the rank the jutsu was cast. Hostiles who attempt to move through this terrain must also pass a Perception check vs your Save DC or take earth damage equal to half of the rank the Jutsu was cast, taking none on a successful save. (D-Rank: 2d4, 20ft, C-Rank: 4d4, 25ft, B-Rank: 6d4, 30ft A-Rank: 8d4 35ft, S-Rank: 10d4, 40ft)
  • Wind Release. You strike forward, creating a spear, bullet or blast of wind targeting a creature you can see in range. Make a ranged Taijutsu attack that cannot be made at disadvantage, dealing your [Unarmed Damage] + wind damage equal to the rank of the jutsu cast. Regardless of a hit or miss, you gain a fly speed equal to your movement speed until the end of your next turn. (D-Rank: 3d8, C-Rank: 5d8, B-Rank: 7d8, A-Rank: 9d8, S-Rank: 11d8)
  • Fire Release. You release a blazing torrent of flame that smolders and explodes forward like a burning missile targeting a creature you can see in range. Make a ranged Taijutsu attack that cannot be made at disadvantage dealing your [Unarmed Damage] + fire damage equal to the rank of the jutsu cast. On a hit, all creatures, except the original target, within a 10-foot radius, centered on the target creature must also make a Dexterity or Constitution (your choice) saving throw, taking the rank-based fire damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. (D-Rank: 2d6+2, C-Rank: 4d6+4, B-Rank: 6d6+6, A-Rank: 8d6+8, S-Rank: 10d6+10)
  • Water Release. You unleash a torrent of crashing waves, overwhelming everyone in your path. All creatures in a 30-foot cone originating from you must make a Strength or Constitution saving throw (your choice). On a failure, you deal your [Unarmed Damage] + cold damage equal to the rank of the jutsu cast blasting the creature to the far edge of the cone, and knocking them prone. Whether they succeed or fail, the overwhelming surge can rip weapons from their grasp, disarming any items they are holding. Structures hit by this jutsu are treated as vulnerable to its damage. (D-Rank: 2d6, C-Rank: 4d6, B-Rank: 6d6, A-Rank: 8d6, S-Rank: 10d6)
  • Lightning Release. You blitz forward with a flash of light and strike with enough kinetic force to penetrate stone, steel and chakra-based defenses in a single strike. Make a melee Taijutsu attack dealing your [Unarmed Damage] + lightning damage equal to the rank of the jutsu cast. If this attack scores a critical hit, you can recast it as a finisher, targeting a different creature. (D-Rank: 3d8, C-Rank: 5d8, B-Rank: 7d8, A-Rank: 9d8, S-Rank: 11d8)

features.name: Elemental Defenses
features.description: While in Elemental Cloak, you gain resistance to your chosen nature release's damage type.

features.name: Primal Glare
features.description: Action in a social encounter: Chakra Control check vs target WIS save — on success, 2 ranks Demoralized and can't lie/withhold info on next question. One-hour cooldown per target.

features.name: Unchecked Power
features.description:
New Martial Techniques:
  • Elemental Core — add 1 Martial Die to a save vs Ninjutsu. Regain the die if its nature release matches your Cloak.
  • Ninjutsu Combo — on Ninjutsu cast: 1 Martial Die to cast using Taijutsu ability mod and gain the Combo keyword plus Finisher-targeting language.
  • Flashguard Counter — on reaction-cast in response to damage: 1 Martial Die → 5×result temp HP (adds to existing jutsu temp HP, but all lost at end of turn).

features.name: Elemental Recharge
features.description: Bonus action, spend 4 Martial Die: gain an additional Action this turn (Taijutsu only). Requires a Full-Turn Action to stabilize before reuse.

features.name: Nin-Tai Technique
features.description: Enter Elemental Cloak at initiative at no Martial Die cost. Difficult terrain doesn't cost extra movement. First time/turn a creature within 10 ft hits you (while you have reaction), they take 4 Martial Die of your Cloak's damage type. On melee Taijutsu/unarmed hits, you may teleport within your speed.

natureReleaseGrants.description: Select one nature release (Earth, Wind, Fire, Water, or Lightning). If you already have multiple releases, the picker excludes ones you already own.

subclasses.name: Passionate
subclasses.description: Relentless combo masters with unbreakable rhythm — green jumpsuits, weighted clothing, and a style built around tempo and Finisher crescendos.

features.name: Never Ending Combo
features.description:
Casting a Taijutsu with the Combo keyword enters DYNAMIC FORM! — until a Finisher Taijutsu or initiative ends. Each Combo cast grants 1 Tempo (max 3, loses 1/turn without a Combo cast). Finisher spends all Tempo for cumulative bonuses:
  • Passive at 1 — Mastery rank in Athletics/Acrobatics base actions; ignore difficult terrain.
  • Passive at 2 — move through enemy spaces; AoOs vs you at disadvantage.
  • Passive at 3 — +1 crit threat & +1 Taijutsu save DC.
  • Finisher 1 — one Martial Technique at no Martial Die cost.
  • Finisher 2 — attack-roll Finisher compares vs all AC in 5 ft (→ 10 at 10, 15 at 17).
  • Finisher 3 — on crit or fail-by-5, regain the Finisher's action.

COMBO EXTENDER (keyword — Tempo Boost)
Taijutsu you cast that have the Combo Extender keyword gain the following effect:
  • Combo Extender. This Taijutsu is treated as a one with the Combo keyword. Also, until the end of your next turn, you can cast one Taijutsu with the Combo keyword using either an Action or Bonus action, ignoring its listed casting time.

features.name: Passionate Flurry
features.description:
Once/turn, in place of an unarmed attack, use Shove/Trip/Grapple. Learn Martial Techniques:
  • Chakra Enhanced Blows — 1 Martial Die: Sustained Pressure or Valve Break without its usual cost; 2+ unarmed hits or Taijutsu save fail inflicts Bruised.
  • Tempo Boost — on Finisher while in Tempo: 1 Martial Die to cast ignoring its casting time, lose Finisher keyword, gain Combo Extender keyword (no finisher effect).

features.name: Unreasonable Training
features.description:
Pick one training implement (second at 14):
  • Binding Hand Wraps — martial weapon (Light, Disarm, Reach 1, Grapple, Unarmed, Hidden); Finishers vs grappled creatures let you maximize up to half-prof bonus' worth of damage dice.
  • Training Weights — bonus action to drop for 1 min: +20 ft speed; first melee hit each turn grants an extra unarmed attack (no ability mod on damage). 10 min re-weighting before reuse.
  • Specialized Training Kit — counts as two chosen toolkits with 5 charges/rest (→ Greater at 10, Superior at 14).

features.name: Dramatic Pose!
features.description: Action (social) / Bonus Action (combat, once/initiative) to strike a pose vs a target — Encourage, Flatter, or Callout. Callout: CHA save vs Taijutsu DC — fail removes benefit of target's most recent successful CHA/WIS check.

features.name: Can't Stop, Won't Stop
features.description: Max Tempo → 5. You no longer must spend all Tempo on Finisher. Passive at 5: advantage on all DEX saves. Finisher at 5: regain CP equal to CP spent this turn.

features.name: Sheer Will
features.description: On a failed save, spend 2 Tempo to remake it. When a creature would make a melee attack against you, spend 2 Martial Die to cast a 1-Action Taijutsu as a reaction.

features.name: Passionate Flame Technique
features.description: On a Finisher Taijutsu, spend 3 Martial Die: gain Tempo Boost's benefits while still applying the Finisher effects.

subclasses.name: Stancer
subclasses.description: Style collectors and stance-blenders — weaving multiple Taijutsu stances so quickly opponents can't predict the rhythm.

features.name: Stance Blending
features.description:
Learn an additional Taijutsu Stance you don't know and its associated Expert feat (feat doesn't raise ability score). At the end of an unarmed/melee Taijutsu attack, you may switch stances in place of a bonus action.

Once/turn when you swap stances, pick a Stance Shift (lasts until your next turn):
  • Impact Switch — push 5 ft on unarmed damage.
  • Defensive Switch — temp HP = 1 Martial Die per unarmed-damage instance.
  • Retaliatory Switch — adjacent HS-jutsu casts trigger a free AoO once/turn (if hit, STR save vs Taijutsu DC — fail prone).
  • Deflective Switch — first damage/turn: reduce by 1 Martial Die.

features.name: Fluid Stance
features.description:
Learn Martial Techniques:
  • Style Shift — reaction to an attack targeting you: 1 Martial Die, switch stance, make an unarmed attack; on hit, STR save or Dazed.
  • Dodge Cancel — on melee Taijutsu/unarmed hit: 1 Martial Die to switch to a defensive stance; next attack by damaged creature against you has disadvantage.

features.name: Secret Stances
features.description:
Pick one clan's modified Taijutsu Stance — your unarmed damage type changes and once/turn inflicts a condition via CON save vs Taijutsu DC:
  • Hanami (Medical Fist) — Necrotic / Envenomed
  • Hyuga (Gentle Fist) — Force / Sealed
  • Uchiha (Agile Fist) — Piercing / Confused
  • Ryu (Dragon Fist) — Chakra / Weakened
  • Kuru (Dark Devotion) — Psychic / Slow (treated as Physical)

features.name: Mimicry of Motion
features.description: Reaction to a social success: Martial Arts check vs target CHA save — on success, use their CHA bonus in place of yours for the rest of the encounter. 1-hour cooldown.

features.name: Stance Mastery
features.description:
Learn another Taijutsu Stance you don't know. New Stance Shift options:
  • Momentum Switch — gain Dash.
  • Anchored Switch — -10 ft speed on unarmed damage.
  • Distracting Switch — hostile damage grants allies advantage on their next attack until your next turn.
  • Resilient Switch — select a damage type, gain resistance until your next turn.

features.name: Ancient Scrolls
features.description: Learn a second clan's Secret Stance. Your original Secret Stance evolves into its Prestige form (powerful subclass-specific riders).

features.name: Stancer Technique
features.description: On a Taijutsu requiring a stance: spend 2 Martial Die. Until end of turn, your attacks/saves ignore jutsu-granted AC bonuses (if attack) and force successful saves to be remade (if save).

subclasses.name: Talented Genius
subclasses.description: Prodigies with instinctive self-weaponization — Perfection-point spenders whose mastery borders on effortless perfection.

features.name: Genius Talent
features.description:
When you roll initiative, enter Flawless state (for the encounter). Once/turn, when you spend 1+ Martial Die, gain 1 Perfection (max 4). Spend on:
  • Flawless Disturbance — once/turn on hostile turn, 2 Perfection: gain an extra reaction (Taijutsu only).
  • Flawless Focus — once/turn, 1 Perfection in place of any Martial Die expenditure (still roll but don't spend).
  • Flawless Stances — once/turn, 1 Perfection to switch stance in place of bonus action; next attack targeting you this round has disadvantage.
  • Flawless Technique — 1 Perfection to roll a Martial Die at advantage (take higher).
  • Flawless Tempo — once/turn on Combo cast: 2 Perfection — next Taijutsu gains Combo Extender and Finisher can be cast as a reaction this turn.

features.name: Focused Genius
features.description:
Learn Martial Techniques:
  • Redirected Aggression — reaction on attack: 1 Martial Die to reduce their attack by result. If missed, make your own unarmed attack using their range; on hit, deal original attack's effects + 1 Martial Die damage.
  • Shatterpoint — on unarmed damage: 1 Martial Die. STR save vs — fail = Shattered.

features.name: Perfected Stillness
features.description: Moment of Stillness techniques cost only 1 Martial Die. The condition inflicted no longer ends at end of target's turn — instead requires a successful resave (not at disadvantage) to end.

features.name: Pressure of the Talented
features.description: In a social encounter, roll 4 Martial Die and halve each result (min 1), recording them. Spend a recorded result to penalize a target's CHA/WIS check. Once per encounter.

features.name: Expanded Talent
features.description:
Max Perfection +2. New spends:
  • Flawless Power — 2 Perfection + bonus action: +2 AC until next turn; first creature saving vs your Martial Techniques suffers penalty = half 1 Martial Die.
  • Flawless Execution — on crit: 1 Perfection to make an additional unarmed attack this action.
  • Flawless Balance — once/turn on forced movement or prone: 2 Perfection to negate (doesn't stop teleportation).
  • Flawless Guard — on hit vs ally within 15 ft: reaction + 1 Perfection, move 10 ft adjacent, reduce their damage by 2 Martial Die.
  • Flawless Clash — once/turn on a Taijutsu Clash check: 1 Perfection to add 1 Martial Die.

features.name: Perfect Physique
features.description: Gain resistance to one damage type (bludgeoning, piercing, slashing, earth, wind, fire, water, lightning, force, poison, or necrotic). Replace by spending a bonus action + 2 Perfection + 2 Martial Die.

features.name: Genius Technique
features.description: On an unarmed/Taijutsu attack dealing unarmed damage: spend 3 Martial Die + 3 Perfection; CON save or Stunned until your next turn.

pickable.editorDescription: Taijutsu Specialist Martial Techniques. Learned via the level-up window. Your subclass also grants additional techniques automatically at level 3.
pickable.pickerDescription: Martial Techniques are learned at levels 1 (×2), 4, 7, 10, 13, 16, and 19 (+1 each). Techniques granted automatically by your Taijutsu Style are added separately and don't count against this pick.

options.name: Arresting Technique
options.description: On a melee unarmed/Taijutsu hit: 1 Martial Die, DEX save — fail: speed 0 and prone until your next turn.

options.name: Delayed Fall
options.description: On falling damage: spend up to 3 Martial Die to reduce the number of damage dice rolled by twice the result.

options.name: Drowsy Strike
options.description: In place of an Attack-action attack: 1 Martial Die. Strike a pressure point — roll Martial Die × 10; if ≥ target's current HP, sleep 10 min (ends on damage / ally wake / being moved). Elite/Solo/higher-level foes immune. Target immune for 8 hours after.

options.name: Forceful Strike
options.description: On unarmed damage: 1 Martial Die, STR save — fail: pushed 5×result ft. 2 dice at 7th, 3 dice at 13th. Colliding with a solid structure deals 1d6 bludgeoning per unmoved 5 ft.

options.name: Martial Guard
options.description: Reaction vs melee attack: 1 Martial Die to reduce the triggering damage by the result. 2 dice at 7th, 3 dice at 13th.

options.name: Parry the Shot
options.description: On hit by a ranged Weapon/Ninjutsu/Taijutsu attack: reaction + up to 3 Martial Die to reduce damage by result + your level.

options.name: Precise Technique
options.description: On Taijutsu cast: up to 2 Martial Die — +1 to hit per die spent.

options.name: Raw Technique
options.description: On Taijutsu cast: up to 2 Martial Die — +1 to save DC per die spent.

options.name: Regenerative Surge
options.description: Bonus Action, 2 Martial Die: regain HP = result + Taijutsu ability mod. Once per rest.

options.name: Shockwave Strike
options.description: On Taijutsu cast requiring an attack: 1 Martial Die. Creatures of your choice within 5 ft of the target make a DEX save; fail = half damage dealt. Once per casting.

options.name: Quivering Strike
options.description: On unarmed hit: 5 Martial Die — target CON at end of each of their turns; fail: 5 Martial Die bludgeoning (unreducible); success ends. Once per rest per target.

options.name: Step of Savagery
options.description: Action + up to 4 Martial Die + full movement in a straight line, passing through creatures. They save DEX; fail = unarmed damage + spent dice, prone, pulled along to your end space. Doesn't affect creatures 2+ sizes larger.

options.name: Step of Silence
options.description: 1 Martial Die: creatures can't react to your movement; no sound or tracks.

options.name: Step of the Stars
options.description: On a triggered attack of opportunity: 1 Martial Die to make two unarmed attacks instead of one.

options.name: Technical Technique
options.description: On Taijutsu cast: 1 Martial Die — gains Technical keyword. Concentration cost is reduced by half the result.

options.name: Hurricane Strikes
options.description: Action, 4 Martial Die: vanish; select up to 4 creatures in movement range, make a single unarmed attack vs each AC, teleport adjacent to one. Hits deal unarmed damage + 4 Martial Die and CON save — fail = Staggered.

options.name: Step of the Wind
options.description: On your turn, 2 Martial Die + all your movement: teleport to a space you can see within your movement range.

options.name: Shatter
options.description: Reaction to a Ninjutsu/Genjutsu casting: 1 Martial Die, move half speed toward caster, make 2 unarmed attacks. On ≥1 hit, Chakra Control check vs your Taijutsu DC — fail Interrupts the casting.

options.name: Unstable Drive
options.description: In place of an AoO attack: 1 Martial Die to cast a D-rank Taijutsu (→ C at 10, B at 17) targeting the trigger.

options.name: Elemental Rush
options.description: On a melee Taijutsu hit: 1 Martial Die + bonus action to cast a D-rank Ninjutsu of your nature release (1-Action cast time) using Taijutsu ability mod. → C at 10, B at 17.

options.name: Elemental Crush
options.description: On a Finisher Taijutsu using its finisher effect: 1 Martial Die to swap to your Elemental Finisher (still consumes chakra). Once/turn.

options.name: Chakra Enhanced Blows
options.description: On an unarmed attack or Taijutsu: 1 Martial Die for Sustained Pressure OR Valve Break at no additional Martial Die cost. 2+ unarmed hits or Taijutsu save fail inflicts Bruised.

options.name: Tempo Boost
options.description: On Finisher while in Tempo: 1 Martial Die to cast ignoring casting time — loses Finisher keyword, gains Combo Extender. No finisher effect applies.

options.name: Style Shift
options.description: Reaction to an attack targeting you: 1 Martial Die, switch Taijutsu stance, make an unarmed attack. On hit, STR save — fail = Dazed.

options.name: Dodge Cancel
options.description: On a melee Taijutsu/unarmed hit: 1 Martial Die to switch to a defensive stance. Next attack by the damaged creature vs you (until your next turn) is at disadvantage.

options.name: Redirected Aggression
options.description: Reaction to a hostile attack: 1 Martial Die — reduce their attack by the result. If the attack misses, redirect: make a melee/ranged unarmed attack using their range; on hit, target suffers their original attack's effects + 1 Martial Die additional damage.

options.name: Shatterpoint
options.description: On unarmed damage: 1 Martial Die — STR save, fail = Shattered.

options.name: Elemental Core
options.description: Add 1 Martial Die to a Ninjutsu save. If the casting jutsu matches your Cloak's nature release, regain the Martial Die at end of turn.

options.name: Ninjutsu Combo
options.description: On Ninjutsu cast: 1 Martial Die to cast using Taijutsu ability mod; gains Combo keyword + Finisher-targeting rider (once/turn, target one affected creature with a Finisher regardless of range, Action or Bonus Action).

options.name: Flashguard Counter
options.description: On a reaction jutsu cast in response to damage: 1 Martial Die — gain temp HP = 5 × result (stacks with jutsu temp HP, all lost at end of turn).

masteryGrants.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.

secondarySubclasses.name: Hallucinatory Instrument
secondarySubclasses.description: Your illusions manifest as an instrument of your choice that everyone can see and hear. Great for crowd-control Auditory Genjutsu.

features.name: Hallucinatory Instrument
features.description: Bonus Action to tune your Hallucinatory Instrument for 1 min. While tuned, you may reshape Auditory Genjutsu that don't require an attack roll to Self (20-ft cone) or Self (10-ft radius sphere). All creatures of your choice in the area are affected (must not be Deafened). 10-min cooldown between tunings.

features.name: Hallucinatory Instrument (2)
features.description:
Pick one (cannot be changed later):
  • Words of Affirmation — Action: allies of your choice in a 10-ft radius gain 2d4 + your level temp HP. Allies can benefit up to twice per initiative.
  • Words of Detriment — Action: creatures of your choice in a 20-ft cone remake resaves (to end suffered effects) at disadvantage.

features.name: Hallucinatory Instrument (3)
features.description: Reaction + 1 Actualization Die when a hostile succeeds a remade saving throw: force them to remake without bonuses, taking the new result.

secondarySubclasses.name: Illusory Weapon
secondarySubclasses.description: You shape your illusions into a psychic weapon — d8 melee / d6 ranged (30 ft) — counting as a Weapon component for Bukijutsu.

features.name: Illusory Weapon
features.description: Bonus Action: summon an Illusory Weapon (design your choice; psychic damage, d8 melee / d6 ranged, 30-ft range, proficient, uses Genjutsu attack bonus). Dismissed at will or when you're incapacitated. Counts as a Weapon (W) component for Bukijutsu of D-Rank (→ C at 7, B at 15). Class features interacting with weapons always treat it as valid.

features.name: Illusory Weapon (2)
features.description: When you take the Attack action with your Illusory Weapon, attack twice instead of once. Your weapon counts as a C-Rank Weapon component.

features.name: Illusory Weapon (3)
features.description: Reaction + 1 Actualization Die when a creature succeeds a save vs a jutsu you or an ally cast: take the Attack action. On ≥1 hit, they remake their saving throw with the new result.

features.name: Illusory Weapon (4)
features.description: Your Illusory Weapon counts as a B-Rank Weapon component.

secondarySubclasses.name: Reality Marble
secondarySubclasses.description: An object of significance you imbue as a focus — store Reality Motes from casts and spend them to relay Genjutsu across multiple targets.

features.name: Reality Marble
features.description: Bonus Action to awaken your Reality Marble (1 min; ethereal glow only you and allowed creatures see). While awake, each Genjutsu you cast stores a Reality Mote. Spend a Mote as part of casting to mark the target as a reality relay — any time you cast a Genjutsu at the marked creature, choose up to 2 additional creatures within 30 ft; each saves as if the original target. Mark ends after; 10-min cooldown per creature. If selected creatures fail, they all share the worst result. 10-min cooldown between awakenings.

features.name: Reality Marble (2)
features.description: Your Genjutsu that require concentration have their concentration costs reduced by 2 (minimum 1).

features.name: Reality Marble (3)
features.description: When a marked relay creature would pass a saving throw, spend 1 Actualization Die to force them to remake — take the new result.

secondarySubclasses.name: Temporal Stopwatch
secondarySubclasses.description: A clock-like focus that bends initiative and reaction economies around your Genjutsu.

features.name: Temporal Stopwatch
features.description:
On initiative, spend and roll 1 Actualization Die; choose to add or subtract from one visible creature's initiative (once per initiative).

Bonus Action to wind/unwind your stopwatch (1 min). For the duration, once per turn when you cast a Genjutsu, also gain Dash or Disengage after the jutsu's effects apply.

features.name: Temporal Stopwatch (2)
features.description:
Pick one (cannot be changed later):
  • Rewind — once/turn on a failed STR or DEX save OR on being hit: Reaction to remake the save or force the attacker to reroll and take the second result.
  • Fast Forward — Bonus Action: target an ally who gains an extra reaction. Per-creature cooldown: once per rest.

features.name: Temporal Stopwatch (3)
features.description: Reaction + 1 Actualization Die when a hostile under a jutsu you or an ally cast attacks or casts: they lose the ability to remake end-of-turn saves until the start of their next turn.

features.name: Actualization
features.description:
Genjutsu you cast and Illusion checks may use Charisma in place of Wisdom for attack, damage, skills, and save DC.

Actualization Die pool = 1 + proficiency bonus (d6, recovers on long rest). When you cast a Genjutsu affecting hostile creature(s):
  • Spend 1 Actualization Die — roll Nd6 equal to the rank of the jutsu (D:1, C:2, B:3, A:4, S:5), add the result to damage.
  • Spend 1 Actualization Die — add result to a Deception / Insight / Perception / Persuasion check.
  • Spend any number — reduce target(s) save by -1 each.

You also gain a rank of Mastery with Illusions at levels 1, 7, and 13 (auto-applied by the sheet).

features.name: Chakra Disruption
features.description:
Once per turn, when you cast a Genjutsu forcing a saving throw (or hit with a Genjutsu attack), all affected creatures' next jutsu this round costs +2 chakra. This penalty scales: +2 at 1, +4 at 5, +6 at 9, +8 at 13, +10 at 17.

At 9th level, when a creature fails a save vs your Genjutsu, spend 1 Actualization Die to force a concentration check (at disadvantage if a concentration check was already required).

features.name: Genjutsu Pledge
features.description: Choose a Pledge (subclass). Features unlock at 2, 6, 10, 14, 18.

features.name: Malleable Mirages
features.description: Learn two Mirages (your pickable resource). Learn +1 at 4 / 8 / 12 / 16 / 20 (total 7 at cap). You may swap a known Mirage when you gain a new one.

features.name: Genjutsu Inception
features.description: Choose one Inception concept. Inceptions function as a secondary subclass — they grant features of their own at 3, 7, and 11 (and 15 for Illusory Weapon). Pick yours via the level-up window; see the 'Genjutsu Inceptions' card in that dialog for each option's details.

features.name: Keen Awareness
features.description: Advantage on History checks (accurate recall of anything seen/heard). Reduce downtime to create or learn Genjutsu by half.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Increase one ability by +1, gain a Feat you qualify for, and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit. Repeats at 8, 12, 16, 19. Cap 20.

features.name: Mind Over Matter
features.description: When you use an Actualization effect on a Genjutsu, that Genjutsu deals additional psychic damage equal to your Genjutsu ability modifier (unless it already adds that mod to damage).

features.name: Mental Refocus
features.description: Once per round, when you succeed a save vs a hostile creature, regain 1 Actualization Die. On short rest, regain 2 (→ 3 at 11, 4 at 17).

features.name: Actualization (2)
features.description: Gain another rank of Mastery in Illusions (auto-applied).

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Second ASI/Feat. See level 4.

features.name: Master of Illusion
features.description:
Pick one of the following now (and another at 15th level):

9th:
  • Higher Understanding — targets must beat your save DC by 8+ to be treated as critically succeeding.
  • Genjutsu Flow — Genjutsu range +30 ft; 5 ft / Touch becomes 30 ft.

15th:
  • New Reality — Genjutsu saves can no longer be made at advantage.
  • Greater Mastery — targets whose save is 4+ lower than your DC treat the result as a critical failure.

features.name: Mental Refocus (2)
features.description: Short-rest Actualization Die recovery increases to 3.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Third ASI/Feat. See level 4.

features.name: Actualization (3)
features.description: Gain another rank of Mastery in Illusions (auto-applied).

features.name: Master of Illusion (2)
features.description: Pick a second Master of Illusion option (see the level-9 entry for the full list).

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Fourth ASI/Feat. See level 4.

features.name: Mental Refocus (3)
features.description: Short-rest Actualization Die recovery increases to 4.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Fifth ASI/Feat. See level 4.

features.name: The Prestige
features.description: Your Genjutsu cannot be dispelled or Interrupted. Once per turn, when you cast a Genjutsu forcing a save, spend 3 Actualization Die to shift all affected creatures' level of success down 1 stage (Critical Success → Success, or Failure → Critical Failure).

subclasses.name: Beguiler
subclasses.description: Confusing adversaries who misdirect foes with illusory duplicates of themselves — up to three Duplicities act as decoys, distractions, and disguised strikers.

features.name: Beguiling Duplicity
features.description:
Bonus Action to summon a Duplicity (intangible visual illusion of yourself) within 15 ft. Lasts 1 min or until dismissed / incapacitated. Up to 3 active; one commanded at a time.
  • Displaced Genjutsu — cast as if from a Duplicity's space (your senses).
  • Illusory Body — AC = Genjutsu DC, 1 HP. Special sight/sense discerns it.
  • Illusory Distraction — melee attacks from hostiles adjacent to both you and the Duplicity target the Duplicity first.
  • Moving Illusion — move a Duplicity up to your speed within 120 ft (part of its summon or a later Bonus Action).

features.name: Beguiling Presence
features.description: When a creature fails a save vs your Genjutsu, you become Invisible to creatures of your choice (among those who failed) until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Who Are You Fighting?
features.description: Reaction + 1 Actualization Die when you would take attack damage and a Duplicity is active: swap places with it — the attack hits the Duplicity instead.

features.name: Question Everything
features.description: Your Genjutsu of D-Rank or lower gain the Unaware keyword (→ C at 10, B at 14).

features.name: Triplicity
features.description: With 2+ Duplicities active: Action + 1 Actualization Die to simultaneously occupy their spaces until end of turn, then dismiss one and teleport to its space. Move As One (spend all movement, move all Duplicities). Act As One (Bonus Action, each Duplicity casts a known Genjutsu of D-Rank or lower — pay only the highest chakra cost).

features.name: Beguiling Force
features.description: Creatures currently under your Genjutsu suffer -1d4 to attack rolls.

features.name: Betray Your Friend
features.description: On Duplicity summon, it transforms into a visible hostile. While within 5 ft of that hostile, attacks vs the Duplicity force the attacker to WIS save vs your Genjutsu DC — fail redirects onto their ally.

features.name: Betray Yourself
features.description: Spend 3 Actualization Die + a Betray Your Friend Duplicity on a creature under your Genjutsu: CHA save vs your DC — fail = Identity Crisis 1 min (resave at end of each of their turns to end).

subclasses.name: Corrupt Thoughts
subclasses.description: Mind-wreckers who inject chakra directly into enemies' thoughts, breaking focus and stealing their will from range.

features.name: Rending Thoughts
features.description:
Action: select a creature within 60 ft. Treated as a Genjutsu of your highest castable rank with Auditory or Visual + Tactile keywords (your choice). WIS save. Success: 1d6 psychic. Failure: 2d6 psychic + 1d4 penalty to next attack roll. Critical Failure: 3d6 psychic + the penalty.

Damage +1d6 and penalty die +1 step at 6 (1d6), 10 (1d8), 14 (1d10).

features.name: Collapsing Your World
features.description: On Genjutsu damage, select one damage die and treat it as its max value. OR when an affected creature fails a save vs your Genjutsu, they become Mentally Compromised for 1 min.

features.name: Nightmare Incarnate
features.description: On psychic damage from a Genjutsu, the next attacker targeting the creature gains +1 to hit and +1 crit threat range (until your next turn).

features.name: Mind Leech
features.description:
Rending Thoughts can be used as a Bonus Action (but not both Action + Bonus same turn).

Spend 1 Actualization Die on Rending Thoughts to enhance effects:
  • Failure — -1 penalty to STR/DEX/CON saves until your next turn (→ -2 at 14, -3 at 18).
  • Critical Failure — same, plus you gain +1 STR/DEX/CON saves (→ +2 at 14, +3 at 18).

features.name: Disruptive Psyche
features.description: Mentally Compromised creatures suffer the condition's enhanced effects.

features.name: Denial is a River
features.description: Spend 1 Actualization Die to use Rending Thoughts at Touch range for an INT save. Success: believe your next reasonable statement for 1 hour. Failure: believe any statement for 24 hours (or until challenged, resave hourly). Critical Failure: belief persists permanently; only a new challenger can trigger a resave. Ends if target enters combat.

features.name: Vindictive Thoughts
features.description: Rending Thoughts affects all creatures of your choice in a 10-ft radius sphere at a point you can see.

features.name: Corrupt Influence
features.description: Mentally Compromised creatures can no longer spend Actions to end hostile conditions (Burned, Corroded, Bruised, etc.). Spend 1 Actualization Die on touch: target gains Mentally Compromised.

subclasses.name: Illusionist
subclasses.description: Phantasm summoners — their illusory companion creature casts their Genjutsu, scouts, and unleashes a Maddening Echo mind-break.

features.name: Phantasm
features.description:
Action to summon your Phantasm (Small illusory ally, 10 HP, uses your Genjutsu DC; stats at feature end in training doc). Command as Bonus Action. Max 1 active (new summon dispels the old). Dispels if >120 ft from you.

Spend 1 Actualization Die to immediately recharge one of its Recharge abilities.

features.name: Illusionary Awareness
features.description: Passive Genjutsu detection = 10 + your Illusions skill bonus. Become aware of Genjutsu on other creatures whose DC is below your passive detection (once/turn in initiative). Always aware of Genjutsu affecting you with the Unaware keyword.

features.name: Phantasmic Evolution
features.description: Phantasm can shift Small/Medium and gains a fly speed = its speed. Reaction when you take damage: swap places if within 20 ft. Once/turn: command Phantasm to cast a D-rank Genjutsu you know (single target even if area/multi-target) using your stats (spends your chakra). → C at 10, B at 14.

features.name: Illusionary Fury
features.description: When your Genjutsu inflicts a Mental Condition, spend 1 Actualization Die to inflict +1 rank.

features.name: Phantasmic Bomb
features.description: On rest, select a C-rank or lower Genjutsu you know that inflicts a Mental Condition — Phantasm gains Thought Bomb (Recharge 9-10 / 1 min), releasing it in a 15 ft radius around its occupied point.

features.name: Phantasmic Double
features.description: You can summon a second Phantasm (both as part of the summon action). Command both individually with a single Bonus Action.

features.name: Distracting Phantasm
features.description: Reaction when a creature within 30 ft of your Phantasm attacks: command the Phantasm to shift into the target's image — attacker WIS save vs Genjutsu DC, fail = must target the Phantasm instead.

features.name: Phantasmic Influence
features.description: Phantasms don't require a Bonus Action to command — they always act on your turn. Can exist up to 1 mile away; dispel returns all visual/auditory info gathered.

subclasses.name: Layered Reality
subclasses.description: Reality-benders who stack Genjutsu on a single target — when enemies think they've escaped one illusion, it's just another layer.

features.name: Alteration of Reality
features.description: Bonus Action + 1 Actualization Die to enter Reality Shift (1 min; ends willingly or on incapacitation). 10-min cooldown after ending.

features.name: False Security
features.description: Your Genjutsu of D-rank or lower can only be Identified by a special sense like Chakra Sight or Chakra Sensing. In Reality Shift, even those fail. → C at 6, B at 14.

features.name: Split Focus
features.description: In Reality Shift, concentrate on up to 3 Genjutsu at once. Reduce concentration cost by 1 (→ -2 at 14).

features.name: The First, Second and Third
features.description: On casting a D-rank or lower single-target Genjutsu requiring a save: 1 or 2 Actualization Die to select additional same-rank non-damaging Genjutsu equal to dice spent, merging them. Cost is increased by each extra jutsu's base. On save fail, target also suffers the merged effects. → C at 14.

features.name: Concealed Unease
features.description: Creatures cannot have advantage on a remake save to end a Genjutsu you are concentrating on.

features.name: Broken Reality
features.description: In Reality Shift, you can change any Genjutsu you cast to use a WIS save.

features.name: Master of Reality
features.description: Once/turn on your turn while in Reality Shift and concentrating on a C-rank or lower Genjutsu: spend 1 Actualization Die to swap its effects with a different concentration Genjutsu you know (applies Failure effects, preserves duration; replaces mental conditions if the new jutsu inflicts any).

features.name: Layered Influence
features.description: Reaction + 1 Actualization Die + end concentration: a creature under a Genjutsu you cast has its current Action Interrupted. Elite Actions lose only the attempt.

subclasses.name: Misty Distortionist
subclasses.description: Inhaled-toxin specialists who fuse poison and illusion into Phantom Vapors — coat weapons, drop clouds, and distort enemies through psychotoxic feedback.

features.name: Phantom Vapors
features.description:
Gain proficiency with Alchemist Kits (if already proficient, gain a rank of Mastery). On rest, convert a Poison/Alchemist kit into a Phantom Vapors Kit with charges = 1 + prof bonus. Recharge: 1 Actualization Die, or 10 min recrafting.

Bonus Action + 1 charge to throw a vial — 10-ft-cube Phantom Cloud for 1 min; Genjutsu targeting creatures inside gain the Inhaled keyword. One active cloud at a time. 10 at +1 charge upsizes to 20-ft cube; 18 at +2 charges heavily obscures and blocks Chakra Sight/Sensing.

features.name: Venom Strike Adept
features.description:
Simple Thrown weapons use your Genjutsu attack bonus and ability mod for damage. Spend 1 Phantom Vapor Charge to coat a simple weapon — damage type becomes poison, unlocks Phantom Weapon + Hallucinatory Flurry abilities (Action). Jutsu cast through these gain Genjutsu + Inhaled keywords. Using either ability removes the poison coat.
  • Hallucinatory Flurry — cast a Bukijutsu you know.
  • Phantom Weapon — pick a known C-rank or lower Genjutsu with 1-Action cast; melee/ranged Genjutsu attack with coated weapon. On hit, deal [Weapons Damage] + the Genjutsu's effects on the target.

features.name: Neurotoxic Feedback
features.description: Reaction + 1 Actualization Die when a creature in your Phantom Cloud takes chakra damage, has jutsu cost increased by a hostile effect, or attempts a Chakra Control check: affected creature CON or WIS save (your choice) vs Genjutsu DC — fail: 4d6 psychic (→ 5d6 at 10, 6d6 at 14, 7d6 at 18) and one of: Blinded, speed halved, or -2 CON saves (until your next turn).

features.name: Toxic Mastery
features.description: Spend 1 Actualization Die: a basic poison vial functions as a specified D-rank-or-lower poison (from the Shinobi Handbook) for 1 hour. → C at 10, B at 18.

features.name: Venom Strike Expert
features.description:
Additional coated-weapon abilities:
  • Venom Chain — two melee/ranged Genjutsu attacks, ammo rolled once; on ≥1 hit, target suffers Psychosis.
  • Nightmare Rain — all creatures of your choice in a Phantom Cloud within 90 ft WIS save; fail = 5d6 chakra + Psychosis (half chakra on success).

features.name: Miasmic Brand
features.description: Allies beginning their turn in / entering your Phantom Cloud gain Miasmic Brand. Branded attacks deal +2d6 poison (twice/turn). If bonus damage lands on a Psychosis creature, Neurotoxic Feedback triggers.

features.name: Phantom Bloom
features.description: Phantom Clouds grow 5 ft in all directions each of your turns (max 40-ft cube). Hostiles beginning/entering on their turn suffer -2 AC until leaving the cloud.

features.name: Venom Strike Mastery
features.description: Coated weapons' damage die +2 steps (max d12). Phantom Weapon can use up to B-rank Genjutsu. Phantom Weapon may strike a second creature in range. Venom Chain and Nightmare Rain become Bonus Actions.

pickable.editorDescription: Malleable Mirages — your pickable class resource. Many require a specific Inception, Pledge, or known Genjutsu. Use + Custom for homebrew.
pickable.pickerDescription: Mirages scale: 2 at level 2, +1 each at 4 / 8 / 12 / 16 / 20 (7 total). You may swap a known Mirage each time you gain a new one.

options.name: Accelerate
options.description: Action: up to 3 willing creatures within 20 ft can Dash as a bonus action for 1 min. Affected creatures can end the effect as a reaction to gain advantage on a DEX save. Per-creature 10-min cooldown.

options.name: Agnostic Mind
options.description: You can understand and speak any language or dialect, so long as the creature you communicate with speaks a language.

options.name: Armor of Psychosis
options.description: Add your Genjutsu ability mod instead of DEX when calculating AC.

options.name: Battle Ready Minds
options.description: Casting Bless can target up to 4 willing creatures within 60 ft instead of the listed range.

options.name: Beast Speech
options.description: Cast Animal Companion as a bonus action at no cost.

options.name: Beguiling Influence
options.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in both Deception and Persuasion. (Apply the ranks manually on the skill rows.)

options.name: Beneath the Boot
options.description: Psionics: Crush can target up to 2 additional creatures.

options.name: Berserk Thoughts
options.description: Shadow Monsters affects all creatures in a 15-ft radius sphere at a chosen point within range.

options.name: Bewitching Whispers
options.description: Charming Dissonance on a Charmed hostile: make yourself the source of infatuation; disposition +1 rank.

options.name: Black Fangs
options.description: Shadow Bite +1 crit threat range; on crit, target saves at disadvantage.

options.name: Blade Song
options.description: Weapons of Darkness cannot lose concentration from damage; no chakra required for concentration.

options.name: Blinding Lights
options.description: Color Spray affects a 10-ft cube at a point within 60 ft; +5 ft per rank above D.

options.name: Book of Stolen Secrets
options.description: In conversation with a scroll/book in hand, ask one question — unwilling targets WIS save vs Genjutsu DC; failure or truthful response writes the most accurate answer in the book. 1-hour cooldown per creature.

options.name: Chained Mind
options.description: Mind Burst: if target within 10 ft, attack at advantage; on save fail, you become Invisible to the Berserked creature.

options.name: Tree Binding Death
options.description: Effortless Paralysis target count up to 3. 10-min rest before reuse.

options.name: Cloak of Chains
options.description: Bonus Action + 1 Actualization Die: phantasmic chakra aura in 5 ft around you, 1 min. Intimidation Mastery vs non-hostiles in aura; hostiles starting a turn in aura take 4 psychic.

options.name: Decelerate
options.description: Action: one creature within 60 ft WIS save vs Genjutsu DC — fail = 1 rank Slow. Target must spend Reaction to resave. 10-min per-target cooldown.

options.name: Demon Sight
options.description: See normally in darkness and chakra-based darkness up to 120 ft.

options.name: Devilish Recovery
options.description: Mind-Body Recovery: spend 2 Actualization Die — temp HP gained becomes HP regained. 1-hour per-target cooldown.

options.name: Doubled Weapon
options.description: Create a second Illusory Weapon (neither can be two-handed). While dual-wielding, damage die -1 step; attacks with them +1 on Attack Action.

options.name: Dreamscape
options.description: Action: target a known creature within 100 miles. Enter trance and appear in their dream (if asleep) — converse, shape dream landscapes. They recall perfectly on waking.

options.name: Dulled Mind
options.description: Slow affects all creatures of your choice in a 20-ft cube at a point within range.

options.name: Evil Thoughts
options.description: Mind Crunch: attack result 10+ over target AC is treated as a crit.

options.name: Fighting Mirage
options.description: Pick a Fighting Style from Chapter 13. Cannot take more than once.

options.name: Gaze of Two Minds
options.description: Action: touch a willing humanoid, perceive through their senses for 1 hour (max range 1 mile; ends if either takes damage). You're Blinded/Deafened to your own senses while active but gain theirs.

options.name: Ghastly Mist
options.description: Fog of War radius becomes 40 ft.

options.name: Grasp of Courage
options.description: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a Genjutsu requiring an attack roll, force them to move up to 10 ft in a straight line closer to you.

options.name: Haunting Melody
options.description: Damaging Auditory Genjutsu through your Hallucinatory Instrument's area: save fails gain 1 rank Demoralized.

options.name: Illusionary Brawler
options.description: Psionics: Strike! on yourself: no chakra to maintain concentration. While affected, unarmed attacks can be Melee Genjutsu attacks.

options.name: Illusionary Vigor
options.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in both Athletics and Acrobatics. (Apply manually on skill rows.)

options.name: Maddening Pain
options.description: Doubled Pain's bonus damage triggers even on ally attacks (once/turn per creature).

options.name: Magnum Opus
options.description: Bringer of Darkness: choose which creatures are affected.

options.name: Misty Thoughts
options.description: Cajolery of Glamour loses HS component. Casting on a non-hostile costs no chakra. Hostiles can't be targeted this way.

options.name: Misty Visions
options.description: Innocuous Aspect can only be dispelled by physical inspection.

options.name: Muddled Movements
options.description: Hitting with a Genjutsu attack reduces target speed by 10 ft until your next turn.

options.name: Myriad Forms
options.description: Transform allows small/medium/large object forms. Large: weight unchanged, occupies original space visually. Small: occupy smaller spaces but cannot move unsupervised. Investigation/Illusion check reveals disguise.

options.name: One with Shadows
options.description: In dim light or darkness, Action to become Invisible and silent. Lost on >½ movement, attack, or jutsu cast.

options.name: Piece of Mind
options.description: Mind Sliver as a bonus action with a d8 damage die.

options.name: Prolonged Songs
options.description: Heavenly Worded Song range becomes Self (40-ft cone).

options.name: Psyche Drinker
options.description: Once/turn, on Genjutsu damage: 1 Actualization Die to gain damage dealt as temp HP until your next turn.

options.name: Relentless Pain
options.description: While within 60 ft of a Doubled Pain target that can see you and you take damage: Reaction + 1 Actualization Die to deal psychic damage equal to the triggering damage.

options.name: Repelling Illusions
options.description: On a Genjutsu attack hit, push the target up to 10 ft in a straight line away from you.

options.name: Shroud of Shadows
options.description: Darkness ninjutsu gains Genjutsu + Visual keywords.

options.name: Signature Work
options.description: Programmed Illusions: choose a known C-rank or lower Genjutsu — the illusion can cast it once per minute using your stats. Dispels if the illusion takes real damage. 1 long-rest cooldown.

options.name: Superior Thoughts
options.description: Cast 1 S-rank Genjutsu you qualify for as a Full-Turn Action, no cost, once per long rest. Gain 3 ranks of Confused + Concussed on use. Repeat costs 4 chakra die.

options.name: Tentative Escape
options.description: Reaction to failing a Genjutsu save: cast Release.

options.name: Vampiric Pain
options.description: Creatures under Doubled Pain take chakra damage = the psychic damage dealt by Doubled Pain.

options.name: Voice of an Old Friend
options.description: Telepathically communicate with 2 willing creatures up to 1 mile away (→ 3 at 7, 4 at 18). Target must be generally known; they can resist.

masteryGrants.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in Illusions.
Gain an additional rank of Mastery in Illusions.
Gain an additional rank of Mastery in Illusions.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
