features.name: Medical Ninjutsu
features.description:
You can learn any jutsu with the Medical keyword. Medical-keyword jutsu may use Wisdom for attack / damage / save DC.

Medicine checks can use Intelligence or Wisdom. You gain a rank of Mastery in Medicine at level 1 (auto-applied), and additional ranks at levels 8 and 12.

features.name: Rejuvenating Rest
features.description: When you and allied creatures regain HP at the end of a short rest, roll 1d6 and add the result to the HP regained. Scales to 2d6 at 5, 3d6 at 10, 4d6 at 15.

features.name: Channeled Healing
features.description: Whenever you cast a Medical-keyword jutsu of D-Rank or higher to restore HP, the affected creature regains +2 additional HP (scales per the Channeled Healing column of the class table). Applies once per turn.

features.name: Tenets of Medicine
features.description: Choose a Tenet (subclass). Features unlock at 2, 6, 10, 14, 18.

features.name: Medical Domain
features.description:
You have 4 Domain Charges (regained on short rest). Each Domain ability costs 1 charge. +1 max charge at 11 and 17.

Base abilities:
  • Preserve Life — on a Medical HP-restoring jutsu: reduce Nd rolled (N = rank) and treat each reduced die as its max. Combat-only; can't target undead/constructs.
  • Sustain Life — Action, touch: willing ally regains CP = 1 Chakra Die + CON mod (die not spent). Combat-only.
  • Take Life — when damaging a creature with a Medical jutsu, increase damage by 5 + your Medical-Nin level.

Subclass-granted Domain abilities add to this list.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Increase one ability by +1, gain a Feat you qualify for, and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit. Repeats at 8, 12, 16, 19. Cap 20.

features.name: Bladed Chakra Scalpel
features.description: Bonus Action: coat your hands in blades of condensed chakra for 1 min. Your Attack-action attacks can instead be Melee Ninjutsu Attacks (Medical keyword, highest rank you can cast) dealing 1d8 + Channeled Healing bonus slashing.

features.name: Mystical Palm
features.description:
Bonus Action: enter Mystical Palm for 1 min (10-min cooldown). While active, with a Medicine Kit:
  • Sterile Channeling — Light Patch Up regains 2d6 per charge instead.
  • Surgical Insight — Full Fix Up costs 1 fewer charge.
  • Pulse Stabilization — Stabilization wakes the target at 1 HP instead of 0.

features.name: Never on the Front Lines
features.description: Within 10 ft of a hostile, you can Dash or Disengage as a bonus action. Dodge also available as a bonus action at 15th level.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Second ASI/Feat. See level 4.

features.name: Medical Ninjutsu (2)
features.description: Gain another rank of Mastery in Medicine (auto-applied).

features.name: Advanced Medical Research
features.description: Learn and create Medical-keyword jutsu in half the time and without lifestyle Ryo. Reduce the final chakra cost of newly created Medical jutsu by its rank (D:1, C:2, B:3, A:4, S:5).

features.name: Medical Domain (2)
features.description: Maximum Domain Charges increases by 1.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Third ASI/Feat. See level 4.

features.name: Medical Ninjutsu (3)
features.description: Gain another rank of Mastery in Medicine (auto-applied).

features.name: Until Their Heart Stops
features.description: A creature other than yourself who regains HP from a jutsu you cast and is currently under a Mental, Physical, or Elemental condition makes their next save or skill check to end it with a rank of Mastery.

features.name: Never on the Front Lines (2)
features.description: Dodge is now also available as a bonus action while within 10 ft of a hostile.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Fourth ASI/Feat. See level 4.

features.name: Medical Domain (3)
features.description: Maximum Domain Charges increases by 1 again.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Fifth ASI/Feat. See level 4.

features.name: Medical Perfection: Supreme Medic
features.description:
You unlock two ultimate Domain abilities (each costs 2 charges):
  • Life Pulse — on a Medical HP-restoring jutsu, affected creatures gain the Life Pulse condition for 1 min. Requires S-Rank-style concentration plus a DC 25 Chakra Control check at the start of each of your turns.
  • Hemorrhage Vitality — on a Medical jutsu that deals damage, affected creatures gain the Hemorrhage Vitality condition for 1 min (S-Rank; can only be removed early by S-Rank jutsu).

subclasses.name: Adept Medic
subclasses.description: Field healers who ascend into a state of heightened support, keeping every ally standing through overflowing HP grants, condition breaks, and vital threading.

features.name: Healing Ascension
features.description: Bonus Action (in combat): enter Ascension state for 1 min. 10-min cooldown.

features.name: Talented Support
features.description:
On a Medical-keyword jutsu cast, pick one:
  • If the target has no conditions, they gain 4 temp HP.
  • If the target has a Physical/Elemental/Mental condition, reduce the jutsu's cost by its rank (D:1, C:2, B:3, A:4, S:5).

features.name: Healing Overflow
features.description: While in Ascension, Overflow Recovery: bonus action + 1 Surge to mark a willing ally with Overflow (1 min / 1 use). The ally can spend it on a save for +1d4. Once per other turn per creature.

features.name: Medical Domain: Mending Spark
features.description: Bonus Action while in Ascension; 1 Charge: touch a willing creature — for 1 min they regain HP equal to your Channeled Healing bonus at the start of their turns. Concentration as C-Rank. One instance of Mending Spark per creature.

features.name: Amplified Ascension
features.description:
While in Ascension, max Healing Surges → 5. New spends:
  • Vital Transference — Action, 2 Surges: mark a hostile within 60 ft; first ally attack damage per turn on them heals the attacker for your Channeled Healing bonus.
  • Condition Break — Action, 2 Surges: release a flash of medical chakra; allies within 20 ft lose all ranks of a single B-Rank-or-lower condition (→ A at 18).

features.name: Gifted Healer
features.description: Treat Wounds: on failure, still recover half the proficient-tier Success Healing. Can affect up to 4 creatures simultaneously. Natural 20 on a Medicine Kit Treat Wounds recovers 2 spent charges.

features.name: Vital Conductor
features.description:
On Medical damage to a hostile OR Medical HP-restoration to an ally (bonus action): mark one affected creature with Vital Thread (1 min; ends if >120 ft). One thread active at a time.
  • Ally — on HP/temp-HP gains from your jutsu, +10 ft movement they can spend immediately (no AoOs). Once per rest per creature. In Ascension, first damage/turn reduced by your Channeled Healing bonus.
  • Hostile — jutsu casts trigger CON save vs your Ninjutsu DC; fail = rank of Slow to end of turn. Success breaks the thread (unless you're in Ascension).

features.name: Over Heal
features.description: Left-over HP restoration on a fully-healed creature converts to temp HP equal to half the spillover. In Ascension, conversion is 100%. Once per 10 minutes per creature.

subclasses.name: Black Medicine
subclasses.description: Poison-and-toxin specialists whose medical chakra has turned deep green with purples and blacks — rending defenses, inflicting Envenomed/Corroded, and detonating their chakra within enemies.

features.name: Toxic Chakra
features.description:
On initiative, you radiate Malignant Aura (1 encounter). Gain Blight resource (max 3): once/turn via bonus action, or by inflicting Envenomed/Corroded with a feature/jutsu.
  • Septic Detonation — Action, 1 Blight: a creature with Envenomed takes 1d12 poison per rank.
  • Rotting Pulse — on a save-based Medical jutsu, 1 Blight: damage becomes Poison or Necrotic; on fail, affected creatures become Blighted.
  • Malignant Barrier — Bonus Action, 1 Blight: shield until your next turn. First melee attacker suffers Corroded.

features.name: Hematoxic Touch
features.description: Medical-keyword jutsu damage +rank (D:1, C:2, B:3, A:4, S:5). On damage, reroll 1s and 2s.

features.name: Poisonous Touch
features.description:
Bladed Chakra Scalpel can deal Acid or Poison. During Scalpel, attacks can be made as a bonus action.

Virulent Dance (Action, 1 Blight): melee Ninjutsu attack with Scalpel vs all creatures within range. On hit, each target CON save — fail = rank Envenomed or Blighted.

features.name: Medical Domain: Plague Doctor
features.description:
On a damaging Medical jutsu: this Domain ability triggers on save-based damage (failed save) or force a CON save (if the jutsu wouldn't). Envenomed/Corroded → -2 on save.

On fail, pick one (two if the target is Blighted):
  • Strip Resistance — target loses one resistance until your next turn.
  • Strip Protection — target DR becomes 0 until your next turn.
  • Strip Fortitude — target's immunity becomes resistance until your next turn.

features.name: Toxic Tongue
features.description: Your poison/acid damage ignores resistance. Immunity to Poisoned and Corroded. Poison Kit crafting checks at advantage.

features.name: Malignant Harmony
features.description:
Max Blight → 5. Upgrades:
  • Septic Detonation (2 Blight) — target exudes 20-ft radius; non-target creatures CON save vs your DC, fail = same damage; success = half.
  • Rotting Pulse (2 Blight) — Envenomed/Corroded saves at disadvantage.
  • Malignant Barrier (2 Blight) — lasts until your next turn; triggers up to twice/turn, inflicting Corroded each time.

features.name: Nerve Shredder
features.description: On damage from a failed save vs your jutsu: target can't take reactions and has disadvantage on attacks until their next turn. Blighted creatures also lose half speed.

features.name: Black Lotus Bloom
features.description: Bloom (Action, 3 Blight): 30-ft aura for 1 min. Creatures of your choice that begin their turn in the aura (or enter it) make a CON save vs your Ninjutsu DC. Fail: at the start of their turns while in aura, they gain 1 rank Envenomed or Corroded, have resistances/immunities suppressed until your next turn, cannot regain HP. Blighted targets save at disadvantage. Creatures who fail once stay failed for 1 min unless they spend a round outside the aura.

subclasses.name: Combat Medic
subclasses.description: Front-line medics who fight alongside the martial specialists — Yin-Seal-powered strikes, Battle Medic stance, and Taijutsu-scale weapon combos backed by medical chakra reserves.

features.name: Martial Competency
features.description: Gain proficiency with Unarmed-property weapons and heavy armor. Bonus-action unarmed attack available. Use WIS in place of DEX for AC. Choose one Taijutsu Stance from Chapter 13.

features.name: Yin Seal: Storage
features.description:
4 Yin Seals (d12); regain 1 on a melee unarmed/Taijutsu crit (once/turn); regain all on 10-min meditation. Bonus Action (in combat): enter Battle Medic for 1 min (10-min cooldown). While in Battle Medic, spend Yin Seals:
  • Crimson Lotus Missile — on a single-attack Taijutsu: 1 Yin Seal adds die to damage; target + creatures within 5 ft (excl. you) STR save vs Taijutsu/Ninjutsu DC — fail = prone + Dazed.
  • Cherry Blossom Rush — Reaction to damage: 1 Yin Seal, roll × 5 ft of movement, then cast a 1-Action Taijutsu at the end.

BATTLE MEDIC (Fortitude Amp)
While in Battle Medic, you gain the following benefits:
  • Your [Unarmed Damage] die becomes a d12.
  • Your unarmed and Taijutsu attacks gain a +1 bonus to their critical threat range. Becomes +2 at 10th level.
  • Taijutsu you cast of D-Rank or lower gain the Medical keyword. Becomes C-Rank at 6th and B-Rank at 10th level.
  • If you begin your turn with half or fewer hit points, you regain hit points equal to your Channeled Healing bonus.

actions.name: Cherry Blossom Rush
actions.description: While in Battle Medic, when you take damage, spend 1 Yin Seal: roll the seal and move that many ×5 ft, then cast a 1-Action Taijutsu at the end of the movement.

features.name: Chakra Enhanced Combat
features.description: Attack twice on the Attack action. On an unarmed crit, you may instead deal [Unarmed Damage] + [Unarmed Damage]. Bladed Chakra Scalpel: unarmed damage becomes Piercing; attack 10+ over target AC = rank Bruised.

features.name: Medical Domain: Strength of 100
features.description:
When entering Battle Medic, spend 1 Domain Charge to enter Strength of 100 — overrides/augments Battle Medic's Yin Seal abilities (see the Tenet Training Doc for the full enhancement list). Default Battle Medic effects apply to anything not replaced.

STRENGTH OF 100 ENHANCEMENT
Replaces / augments the default Battle Medic effects with:
  • Your [Unarmed Damage] die becomes 2d12.
  • You gain a rank of Mastery with Strength based checks and saves.
  • Constructs and Structures are Vulnerable to your [Unarmed Damage].
  • Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack that deals [Unarmed Damage], you can push the target 10 feet in any direction except upwards.

features.name: Expert Combatant
features.description: Max Yin Seals → 6. Unarmed gains Deadly 2. Unarmed/Taijutsu attacks ignore up to 4 DR. When a creature within 5 ft misses you with a melee attack, gain advantage on your next melee attack vs them.

features.name: Yin Seal: Burst
features.description:
Spend Yin Seals:
  • Iron Rose Spear — in place of an Attack-action attack, 2 Yin Seals: melee Taijutsu attack; on hit, [Unarmed Damage] × 2 + 2d12; STR save — fail = pushed 30 ft + Staggered.
  • Magnolia Hammer — in place of an Attack-action attack, 2 Yin Seals: 15-ft radius DEX save. Fail = [Unarmed Damage] × 2 + 2d6 + prone + Dazed; success = half damage.

features.name: Medical Domain: Strength of 1000
features.description:
Entering Battle Medic: 1 Domain Charge + 2 Yin Seals to enter Strength of 1000. Augments Battle Medic further (see Tenet Training Doc). Default Battle Medic effects apply to anything not replaced.

STRENGTH OF 1000 ENHANCEMENT
Replaces / augments the previous Battle Medic / Strength of 100 effects with:
  • Your [Unarmed Damage] die becomes 2d12.
  • You gain 2 ranks of Mastery with Strength based checks and saves.
  • Constructs and Structures are Vulnerable to your [Unarmed Damage].
  • Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack that deals [Unarmed Damage] you can push the target 10 feet in any direction except upwards.
  • You gain DR (damage reduction) vs all Damage equal to your Channeled Healing bonus.

features.name: Yin Seal: Release
features.description:
Max Yin Seals → 8. Spend Yin Seals:
  • Tiger Lily Drive — Action, 3 Yin Seals: 60-ft line; each creature DEX save — fail = [Unarmed Damage] × 3 + 3d6 + pushed 20 ft + prone + full-movement to stand; success = half.
  • Camellia Rupture — in place of an Attack-action attack, 3 Yin Seals: melee Taijutsu; on hit, [Unarmed Damage] × 3 + 3d12, ignore resistance, immunity → resistance; CON save — fail = Stunned until their next turn.
  • Orchid Pulse — Bonus Action, 3 Yin Seals: allies in 20-ft aura gain 2× Channeled Healing bonus as temp HP, end a condition of your choice, and gain immunity to Bruised/Bleed/Dazed/Slowed/Blinded/Stunned until your next turn.

resources.name: Yin Seals
resources.maxFormulaDescription: 4 at L2, 6 at L10, 8 at L18

subclasses.name: Reaper
subclasses.description: Pact-bound medics who summon fragments of the Reaper's power — Grim-prefix jutsu, Curse of the Reaper, and hex-driven combat with power/polearm/scythe weapons.

features.name: Reapers Techniques
features.description:
Learn Grim-prefix jutsu via dark Fuinjutsu (don't count against known jutsu). Grim jutsu of D-rank or lower with 1-Action cast time can be cast as a bonus action (not also as an Action the same turn). → C at 10, B at 18.

Expanded Jutsu by level:
  • 2nd — Grim Mask, Grim Weapon
  • 6th — Grim Blade, Grim Legion
  • 10th — Grim Calling
  • 14th — Grim Reality

features.name: Reapers Arsenal
features.description:
Proficiency with Martial Weapons from Power/Polearm groups + Kama/Kusarigama/Scythe/Triple-Bladed Scythe (these lose Blade grouping; count as both Power & Polearm). Pick a Bukijutsu Stance (Power, Great Weapon, Polearm, or Two-Weapon).

Curse of the Reaper — on damage to a creature with a weapon attack or a Grim jutsu, mark them as Cursed for 1 min. One curse active at a time. Pick a Hex effect (see the Curse writeup).

features.name: Entropic Combatant
features.description:
Attack twice on the Attack action. Bladed Chakra Scalpel can coat a wielded weapon — drop ability-mod-to-damage in favor of Channeled Healing bonus; damage becomes Slashing.

Once/turn when casting a Chain-keyword Bukijutsu targeting a Cursed creature, auto-benefit from Chain 1 or Chain 2 effects regardless of current chain rank.

features.name: Medical Domain: Reapers Verdict
features.description:
1 Domain Charge: mark a creature within 60 ft as Gravetouched (1 min or until death). Gravetouched = Cursed with a Hex (pick one); doesn't end on a new Curse of the Reaper.

On [Weapon Damage] or Grim-jutsu damage vs a Gravetouched creature, apply the Hex's bonus effect:
  • Vitality Hex — allies within 20 ft regain HP = your Channeled Healing bonus.
  • Tributing Hex — target's next jutsu cost +Channeled Healing bonus (until your next turn).
  • Withering Hex — target's penalties apply to your jutsu too.

features.name: Jashin's Harvest
features.description: Reaction when a Cursed or Gravetouched creature casts a jutsu within 15 ft: wave of necrotic chakra — creatures within 15 ft of the trigger CON save vs Ninjutsu DC; fail = 7d6 chakra damage (9d6 at 18); success = half. On ≥1 fail, allies within 20 ft regain CP = Channeled Healing bonus. All Curse/Gravetouched instances dispel after use (reapply normally).

features.name: Hexing Expansion
features.description:
Additional Hex options when applying Curse of the Reaper or Gravetouched:
  • Devouring Hex — target's first jutsu each turn costs +Channeled Healing bonus; max concentration jutsu -1.
  • Executioner's Hex — Jashin's symbol; when you take damage, target takes necrotic = result (ignores resistance & immunity).

features.name: Soul Draining
features.description: Once/turn when casting a damaging Grim jutsu, you regain HP = half the damage dealt (half the total rolled for multi-target effects).

features.name: Avatar of the Reaper
features.description:
Up to 2 creatures Cursed simultaneously (shared Hex). Cursing a 3rd replaces the oldest. Bonus Action once/turn: swap the active Hex across all cursed/Gravetouched creatures.

New Hex — Reapers Hex: target's HP/CP/temp gains go to you instead. On drop to 0 HP or CP, 15-ft burst — enemies CHA save for 6d6 necrotic + chakra (half on success); allies regain 4d6 HP regardless.

subclasses.name: Transmuter
subclasses.description: Cellular-alteration specialists — biology benders who reshape allies and enemies through Transfiguration designations (Lion, Dog, Bear, etc.) and chimeric upgrades.

features.name: Transfigured Technique
features.description:
Bonus Action, touch: apply Transfiguration.
  • Willing ally — 1 min (only one ally Transfigured at a time; new application replaces the oldest).
  • Hostile — CON save vs Ninjutsu DC; fail = Transfigured until end of your next turn. Re-applying extends the duration by 1 round.

Pick a biological designation on apply; effects run for the duration or until you re-apply Transfiguration to the target.

TRANSFIGURATION (Special Amp — Fortitude)
You are temporally Transfigured, having your cells, organs and biological makeup slightly adjusted to better match one of the following designations chosen by the allied Medical-Nin that applied this effect:
  • Avian. You gain a flying speed equal to your movement speed, but must land at the end of your turn. You gain immunity to fall damage and cannot be knocked prone.
  • Carnivoran. You gain a bonus to your [Unarmed Damage] and [Weapon Damage] equal to the Medical-Nin's Channeled Healing bonus.
  • Herbivore. You gain DR equal to the Medical-Nin's Channeled Healing bonus.
  • Insectoid. You gain Darkvision and Chakra Sight out to 60 ft and resistance to Chakra Damage.

features.name: Condition Override
features.description:
When a Medical-keyword jutsu would inflict a Physical or Elemental condition, you may swap it for Weakened or Bruised instead (declare on cast).

Transfigured creatures can't save at advantage against conditions from your Medical-keyword jutsu.

features.name: Transmutation
features.description:
Bladed Chakra Scalpel damage can become Necrotic; range becomes 10 ft. Once/turn on scalpel damage, target CON save vs Ninjutsu DC — fail = rank Weakened + Transfigured.

While Mystical Palm is active, Transfigured allies can benefit from two biological designations instead of one.

features.name: Medical Domain: Transmuted
features.description:
Bonus Action, 1 Charge: pulse of biotransmutative chakra in 20 ft. Until your next turn, allies in the radius gain ONE of:
  • Lion — rank of Mastery to attacks + 1 crit threat range.
  • Dog — +2 AC while within 10 ft of another ally.
  • Bear — advantage on STR and CON saves.
  • Deer — advantage on INT and WIS saves.
  • Rabbits — advantage on DEX and CHA saves.

features.name: Chimeric Mutation
features.description:
Transfiguration unlocks additional designations for allies or enemies (chimeric/otherworldly options).

CHIMERIC MUTATION — TRANSFIGURATION

Enhancement (applied to allies):
  • Aberration. You gain resistance to psychic damage and immunity to the Mental conditions.
  • Demonic. Once per turn, you gain a bonus to your jutsu-based damage equal to the Medical-Nin's Channeled Healing bonus.
  • Monstrosity. You have advantage on the first roll of a d20 made each turn with regards to checks and attacks.

Penalty Options (applied to hostiles):
  • Aberration. A transfigured (Aberration) creature suffers disadvantage on all checks relying on any of their 5 senses and they cannot benefit from jutsu-based bonuses.
  • Demonic. A transfigured (Demonic) creature becomes unstable; the first time each turn they take damage, they must make a Constitution saving throw vs the Medical-Nin's Ninjutsu save DC, being Dazed on a failed save.
  • Monstrosity. A transfigured (Monstrosity) creature loses control of their own bodily functions, wanting to attack everything around it. If more than 1 creature is within 10 feet of it, it suffers a -1 penalty to hit for every creature within 10 feet of it.

features.name: Metachirurgical Mastery
features.description: When a Medical-keyword jutsu would inflict a Physical condition, you may swap it for the Transfiguration condition instead.

features.name: Transmuted Self
features.description: Applying Transfiguration to yourself, you may select up to 2 benefits. Once per 10 minutes.

features.name: Transmogrified Biology
features.description:
Applying Transfiguration to a hostile, you may maintain it as concentration (up to 1 min) at no chakra cost — instead of ending at the end of your next turn.

Unlock the Sage Beast designation.

TRANSMOGRIFIED BIOLOGY — TRANSFIGURATION

Enhancement:
  • Sage Beast. At the start of each of your turns, you regain hit points equal to the Medical-Nin's Channeled Healing bonus. If you are at full hit points, you instead gain temporary hit points equal to the same value. You also gain a +2 bonus to your critical threat range, and once per turn you can spend half your movement to select a space you can see within 60 feet that can hold you, teleporting to the chosen space.

Penalty Options:
  • Sage Beast. A transfigured (Sage Beast) creature suffers uncontrollable chakra overflow and physical instability.
    • At the beginning of a transfigured creature's turn, they take necrotic damage equal to the Medical-Nin's Channeled Healing bonus as their cellular structure continues to break down.
    • When the affected creature casts a jutsu, they must roll a d20. On a roll of 5 or more, their jutsu cast has its cost increased by an amount equal to the Medical-Nin's Channeled Healing bonus.

masteryGrants.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in Medicine.
Gain an additional rank of Mastery in Medicine.
Gain an additional rank of Mastery in Medicine.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.

resources.name: Medical Domain Charges
resources.maxFormulaDescription: 4 at L3, 5 at L11, 6 at L17

features.name: Weapon Focus
features.description:
Pick a weapon group to specialize in: Blade, Flail, Polearm, Power, or Thrown/Ammunition. You gain proficiency with Exotic weapons of the chosen group; those weapons are now your Weapon Focus.

While wielding your Weapon Focus:
  • Bukijutsu cast with it can use DEX in place of STR for Taijutsu ability.
  • Martial Arts checks can use DEX in place of STR.

Additionally, you can enhance your Weapon Focus with a bonus property from the Weapon Specialist table (Blocking 2 max, Deadly 3 max, Disarm melee-only, Finesse melee-non-two-handed, Multiattack, Reach melee-only, Thrown+Returning, or Trip). Select another property at 7th and 13th levels.

features.name: Discipline
features.description:
Pool of 2 Discipline (recovered on short/long rest, and once/turn on a Chain-keyword Bukijutsu you gain Discipline equal to the Chain rank you benefit from).

Discipline powers Flurry Techniques rolled with d12 Flurry Die. You begin with three Flurry Techniques:
  • Deflection — Reaction, 1 Discipline: reduce damage by 1 Flurry Die (2 at 11, 3 at 20).
  • Chained Momentum — once/turn, 1 Discipline as part of a non-Chain Bukijutsu cast: gain Chain keyword + maintain current chain value. Alternatively, on a Chain cast, 2 Discipline to bump or drop chain rank by 1.
  • Chakra Strike — once/turn on [Weapon Damage] from a Bukijutsu: 1 Discipline, +1 Flurry Die to first damage instance (+2 at 5, +3 at 11, +4 at 15, +5 at 20; area jutsu roll half dice, min 1).

features.name: Weapon Stance
features.description: Choose one Bukijutsu Stance from Chapter 13 and gain its associated Bukijutsu Stance Expertise.

features.name: Katas
features.description: Pick one Kata (your pickable resource). You gain more at later levels per the class table.

features.name: Augmentation
features.description:
Select one Augmentation (add another at 9 and 15):
  • Tactical — INT/WIS skill checks at advantage (1 Discipline).
  • Psyche — WIS/CHA saves vs Genjutsu / mental effects at advantage (2 Discipline).
  • Mobile — roll 1 Flurry Die for +5×result speed, Dash as bonus action (1 Discipline).
  • Sensory — gain 30 ft of Tremor sense or Blindsight (1 Discipline; out-of-combat: 1 min, in-combat: pick both until next turn).
  • Restorative — bonus action: regain HP = 1 Flurry Die (2 at 9, 3 at 15). Combat only; next-turn cooldown.
  • Enduring — STR/CON saves at advantage (2 Discipline).

features.name: Eye for Skill
features.description: Bonus Action: select a creature within 30 ft and make a Martial Arts check vs their Level DC (Mastery rank bonus if you've dealt damage this turn). On success, learn one of: AC, a condition immunity/resistance, damage immunity/resistance, highest or lowest ability score, a trait, or a special action they can take. On failure, can't reuse on the same target until scoring a hit next turn.

features.name: Combat Doctrine
features.description: Choose a Combat Doctrine (subclass). Features unlock at 3, 6, 10, 14, 17.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: Increase one ability by +1, gain a Feat you qualify for, and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit. Repeats at 8, 12, 16, 19. Cap 20.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: Attack twice on the Attack action. Additionally, taking Dash or Disengage as an Action lets you make one weapon attack as part of that action.

features.name: Critical Focus
features.description: Weapon Focus weapons gain +1 rank of the Critical property. Critical property now applies to Taijutsu attacks with the Weapon Focus. +1 additional rank at 11 and 18.

features.name: Chaining Adept
features.description:
Once per turn on a Chain-keyword Bukijutsu cast, gain a benefit based on rank:
  • Chain 1 — remake one missed attack per casting.
  • Chain 2 — reduce cost by rank (D:1, C:2, B:3, A:4, S:5).
  • Chain 3 — allies within 10 ft gain temp HP = 1 Flurry Die (2 at 11, 3 at 15); you gain half as temp HP.

features.name: Inspiring Words
features.description: In combat, Action + 2 Discipline: plant your Weapon Focus; allies within 30 ft regain HP = 1 Flurry Die + one of their Hit Die (doesn't spend the die).

features.name: Weapon Focus (2)
features.description: Select another bonus property for your Weapon Focus.

features.name: Superior Discipline
features.description:
Select 2 benefits (add 1 more at 13 and 18):
  • Redirect — Deflection also deals damage equal to reduction, same type as your weapon.
  • Warden — Deflection can target an ally within 10 ft.
  • Persistence — Deflection usable without a reaction (once/turn if so).
  • Impulse — Chained Momentum usable on any non-Chain jutsu (not just Bukijutsu).
  • Stimulus — Chained Momentum grants temp chakra = 2 × current Chain rank.
  • Blitz — Chakra Strike free (no Discipline cost) at half Flurry Die count.
  • Assault — Chakra Strike as part of Attack action: 1 Flurry Die applied to every attack.
  • Raid — Chakra Strike can be applied to an ally's Taijutsu/Bukijutsu via reaction.

features.name: Augmentation (2)
features.description: Select another Augmentation.

features.name: Flurry Attack
features.description: On Attack action, 2 Discipline: one additional attack as part of the action. Additionally, 2 Discipline to use the Multiattack weapon property once per turn without a bonus action.

features.name: Critical Focus (2)
features.description: Weapon Focus gains another +1 rank of Critical.

features.name: Chakra Sunder
features.description: Reaction + 3 Discipline when you see a hostile within 60 ft casting a jutsu targeting you: cast a 1-Action Bukijutsu with the Clash keyword to initiate a Clash. Your check is at advantage if your cast rank is ≥ the triggering jutsu's.

features.name: Weapon Focus (3)
features.description: Select another bonus property for your Weapon Focus.

features.name: Superior Discipline (2)
features.description: Select another Superior Discipline benefit.

features.name: Augmentation (3)
features.description: Select another Augmentation.

features.name: Critical Focus (3)
features.description: Weapon Focus gains another +1 rank of Critical.

features.name: Superior Discipline (3)
features.description: Select another Superior Discipline benefit.

features.name: Dauntless
features.description:
You regain 1 Discipline at the start of each of your turns. Discipline costs of Katas reduce by 1 (min 1).

Allies within 15 ft: the first save failure per ally per rest, you can spend 2 Discipline to allow them to remake the saving throw. One use per ally per rest.

subclasses.name: Battle Dancer
subclasses.description: Ruthlessly efficient duelists who turn combat into choreography — switching between Samba and Waltz dances to overwhelm foes through mobility and flourish.

features.name: Dazzling Dances
features.description:
Bonus Action in combat: enter Samba or Waltz. Dance for the duration of combat (end early no action). Bonus Action to switch.

SAMBA (Battle Amp)
You move rhythmically, with a swift and decisive dance, making it hard to foresee your next move. While in this dance:
  • Whisk. As part of dealing damage with a Bukijutsu, you can spend 1 Discipline. You can reroll the damage dealt, taking the highest result.
  • Spin Step. As part of a weapon attack, you can spend 2 Discipline to make the attack target all hostile creatures within your weapon's range. If your weapon is a ranged weapon or has the Thrown property, you instead target all hostile creatures within 15 feet of you. Compare your attack result to the AC of all targeted creatures, scoring a hit on a success.
  • Technical Step. Once per turn, as a bonus action you can spend 2 Discipline to perform a special sequence while dancing. The next attack you make is at advantage. If this attack scores a critical hit you gain an additional action this turn.

WALTZ (Battle Amp)
You perform a smooth dance filled with elegant steps, but you can't do it alone. You require a Partner. At the beginning of your turn you select a Partner — a willing creature whom you can see who remains your Partner until the beginning of your next turn. You can only have 1 Partner at a time. When you begin your turn within 10 feet of your willing Partner, you both gain the following benefits:
  • Move in Tandem. If you or your Partner goes first in the turn order, either of you can spend 1 Discipline to allow the other to take their turn at the same time, on the same initiative count this round. When their turn comes up in the initiative count, it is skipped until the next round. If a Partner without access to Discipline uses this ability, they can spend 1 of the Weapon Specialist's Discipline to use this ability.
  • Battle Step. In place of your movement, you and your partner select two spaces adjacent to each other that either of you can reach with your movement and perform an aggressive maneuver, moving to the chosen spaces. This spends all of the user's movement.
  • Cascade Step. When you take the Attack action or cast a jutsu that requires an attack, you can spend 1 Discipline to force the affected creature to make a Dexterity saving throw vs the Weapon Specialist's Taijutsu Save DC. On a failure, they suffer a -2 penalty to their AC until the beginning of your next turn. If a Partner without access to Discipline uses this ability, they can spend 1 of the Weapon Specialist's Discipline to use this ability.
  • Defensive Dance. When you or your Partner take damage you can spend your Reaction and 2 Discipline to gain resistance to the triggering damage. If a Partner without access to Discipline uses this, they can instead spend their reaction and 1 of the Weapon Specialist's Discipline to use this ability.

actions.name: Technical Step
actions.description: While in Samba, once per turn spend 2 Discipline: your next attack is at advantage; if it's a critical hit, gain an additional action this turn.

actions.name: Defensive Dance
actions.description: While in Waltz, when you or your Partner take damage, spend 2 Discipline (or 1 if a Partner without Discipline triggers it): gain resistance to the triggering damage.

features.name: Flourish!
features.description:
Learn Flurry Techniques usable while wielding weapons with Thrown/Finesse/Reach/Multiattack/Light/Ranged:
  • Echo Step — on hit, 1 Discipline: push target 10 ft and move 10 ft (no AoOs). Waltz: Partner also moves and attacks. Samba: also prone or disarm.
  • Shifting Rhythm — on turn start with Physical/Mental condition, 2 Discipline + 1 Flurry Die. 10+: remove all ranks; otherwise remove 1 rank. Waltz: use on Partner. Samba: also works with Elemental conditions.
  • Inspiring Flourish — in place of attack or Bukijutsu, spend any Discipline: each Discipline grants an ally a special reaction (move / 2 attacks / D-rank jutsu). Waltz: Partner gets 2 reactions on different activities. Samba: C-rank at base, B-rank at 14.

features.name: Twin Step
features.description:
On a save-forcing Bukijutsu, 3 Discipline:
  • Waltz — Partner makes a weapon attack vs target before save; on hit, save at disadvantage.
  • Samba — cast a second same-rank-or-lower Bukijutsu forcing a different ability save. Target saves vs both simultaneously; failing either = suffers both.

features.name: In the Spotlight
features.description: Reaction on a hostile's turn in a social encounter + 1 Discipline: take the Spotlight. Roll 1 Flurry Die per CHA/WIS check they make; 7+ = disadvantage.

features.name: Spectacular Form
features.description:
Samba: Encore (max [Weapon Damage] die → 1 Discipline to roll another; recursive) + Blazing Rhythm (on hit, 1 Discipline per successful attack: +1 to subsequent attack rolls, stacks to +3, end of turn).

Waltz: up to two different Partners.

features.name: Whirlwind Pirouette
features.description: Action: spend X Discipline + select X creatures within 15 ft. Single melee Taijutsu attack as if B-rank Bukijutsu vs each AC. On hit, [Weapon Damage] + Chakra Strike damage. Failed STR save = prone + 3 ranks Dazzled.

features.name: Dance of the Dawn
features.description:
Bonus Action, 4 Discipline: enter Samba AND Waltz for 1 min. Pick 2 benefits:
  • Guiding Rhythm — Partners starting within 10 ft get +1 attack & crit threat.
  • Rejuvenating Step — Discipline spends heal you and Partners 4 HP each.
  • Spotlights Call — Partners can reaction-redirect a save / attack vs them (unless they're the target).
  • Synchronous Steps — B-rank-or-lower casts by you or a Partner triggers other Partners' reaction casts targeting the same creature.

subclasses.name: Fang Breaker
subclasses.description: Savagely furious polearm/flail wielders — wide-arc Battle Trance strikers who dismantle defenses piece by piece.

features.name: Fang Breaker Fury
features.description:
Bonus Action, 1 Discipline: enter Battle Trance (1 min or until unconscious). 10-min meditation cooldown; re-enter early costs +1 Discipline per prior use.

BATTLE TRANCE (Battle Amp)
While in Battle Trance you gain the following benefits:
  • Furious Advantage. You have advantage on checks and saving throws using your Taijutsu ability score.
  • Furious Resilience. You have resistance to one damage type of your choice (chosen when you enter Battle Trance). Increases to two choices at 10th level and three choices at 17th level.
  • Furious Focus. You cannot maintain concentration on non-Taijutsu or Bukijutsu based effects for the duration of your Battle Trance.

features.name: Savage Assault
features.description:
Flurry Techniques usable with Polearm or Flail weapons:
  • Okuri-Inu Fang — on [Weapon Damage], 2 Discipline: affected creatures STR save; fail = Shaken.
  • Hachiman's Brutality — on [Weapon Damage], 2 Discipline: affected creatures CON save; fail = Hobbled.
  • Koyanes Awareness — Bonus Action, 1 Discipline: 60 ft Fracture Sense until end of next turn; aware of creatures with Physical/Mental/Elemental conditions.

features.name: Berserkers Frenzy
features.description:
In Battle Trance, gain all Level-6 Battle Trance benefits. Immunity to Charmed and Demoralized.

BATTLE TRANCE — Level 6 Benefits (Battle Amp upgrade)
While in Battle Trance you also gain:
  • Frenzied Assault. When you deal damage with an attack or Bukijutsu that uses Polearm or Flail weapons, you add +2 damage. Increases to +4 at 14th level. At the start of your turn, you can forgo this damage bonus to gain advantage on the first attack you make using a Polearm or Flail weapon.
  • Frenzy Roar. When you take damage from a creature within 15 feet of you, you can spend your reaction and 2 Discipline to release a roar that shakes the battlefield. The triggering creature and all other hostile creatures within 15 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw, gaining 2 ranks of Demoralized on a failed save, or 1 rank on a successful save.
  • Undying Frenzy. When you would fall to 0 hit points, you can spend 3 Discipline to instead fall to 1 hit point. When you do, you end your Battle Trance state. You cannot use this ability again for 10 minutes.

features.name: Gut Feeling
features.description: Exploration/social: 3 Discipline to ask the GM a single-word impression: Safe, Risky, Trap, Honest, Deceptive, Warning, Advantageous, or Exposed.

features.name: Savage Exploits
features.description:
Additional Flurry Techniques:
  • Okuninushi Frenzy — crit + 2 Discipline: CON save; fail = Stunned to end of next turn.
  • Raijin's Terror — crit + 2 Discipline: disadvantage on INT/WIS/CHA saves and one-concentration limit for 1 min (CON save at end of each turn ends).
  • Fujin Cleave — on [Weapon Damage], 2 Discipline: same damage to adjacent creatures of your choice.
  • Shuten-Doji Dismantling — on [Weapon Damage], X Discipline: X creatures suffer -1d4 to next save/attack.

features.name: Aggressive Reprisal
features.description: In Battle Trance: on taking damage, gain 1 Temporary Discipline (max 4, lost at start of next turn). Spendable as normal Discipline. On hostile damage within weapon range, Reaction + 2 Discipline: cast a C-rank 1-Action Bukijutsu vs them.

features.name: Titanic Wrath
features.description: Battle Trance doesn't need a bonus action (enter at start of turn). No cooldown between uses. Savage Exploits Discipline costs -1. On a failed Taijutsu-ability save, 1 Discipline to treat it as a success (escalating +1 per prior use before short rest).

subclasses.name: Obsidian Guard
subclasses.description: Unbreakable defenders — heavy flail/hammer wielders with an Obsidian Ward that intercepts damage and a Provoke-Protect loop that anchors the battlefield.

features.name: Obsidian Ward
features.description:
Summon an Obsidian Ward with HP = 11 + your level. Intercepts all damage to you (before temp HP); carries over if reduced to 0. Shares your resistances/immunities/DR.

Ward Restoration — once/turn on your turn, spend Discipline: ward regains 1 Flurry Die HP (2 dice at 10, 3 dice at 17). Ward regains full HP on any rest.

features.name: Warden Techniques
features.description:
Flurry Techniques usable with Power/Flail weapons or a weapon with Blocking:
  • Provoke — in place of attack or bonus action, 1 Discipline: target within 30 ft (sees or hears you) becomes Provoked until your next turn. +1 target at 6, +1 at 14.
  • Obsidian Projection — Reaction when an ally within 15 ft takes damage, 1 Discipline: redirect to your ward (excess carries to you, not ally). Can't use if ward at 0.
  • Punishing Guardian — on [Weapon Damage] to a creature that damaged an ally since your last turn, 2 Discipline: STR save; fail = 2 ranks Concussed (1 on success).

features.name: Aura of Protection
features.description: While ward has HP, allies of your choice within 15 ft gain +1 AC and saves (+2 at 14). Ward can regain HP even off-turn (still once/turn).

features.name: Retributive Words
features.description: Reaction + 2 Discipline when a creature makes a CHA check against an ally: Intimidation check (use Taijutsu ability mod) contested by their roll. On success, nullify the benefit.

features.name: Obsidian Bulwark
features.description:
Ward max HP +proficiency bonus. Flurry Die rolls to restore ward can roll twice, take higher.

Additional Flurry Techniques (Power/Flail/Blocking):
  • Stonewall Reprisal — on ward damage, 2 Discipline + Reaction: until next turn, each ward-damaging creature triggers a weapon attack from you; [Weapon Damage] adds to ward HP.
  • Shackle Guard — Reaction when an ally within 15 ft takes damage, 2 Discipline: move 10 ft toward damager and attack; on hit, [Weapon Damage] + 2 Flurry Die + STR save for Slowed + Prone.
  • Intimidating Presence — Bonus Action, 2 Discipline: all hostiles in Aura of Protection become Provoked until next turn.

features.name: Guardian Force
features.description: In combat at turn start: 1 Flurry Die added to ward HP. On ally STR/DEX/CON save within Aura of Protection, 2 Discipline: intercept the effect to yourself.

features.name: Boundless Guardian
features.description: Extra reaction, usable only for Obsidian Guard features. Provoked lasts until a hostile targets/damages you. Provoked creatures take +1 Flurry Die damage from your allies starting their turns in Aura of Protection.

subclasses.name: Primal Weapon
subclasses.description: Elemental-chakra wielders who manifest a Primal Weapon of pure nature-release energy and chain it with Ninjutsu for devastating hybrid combos.

features.name: Elemental Armaments
features.description: Focus your innate element. Manifest or dismiss a Primal Weapon (no action, once/turn) — takes your Weapon Focus group's shape, uses that weapon's statistics, damage type becomes your nature release. Light Primal Weapons can dual-summon. Infuse Weapon Seals on long rest: 2 D-rank at 3 (+1 C at 6, +1 B at 10, +1 A at 14, +1 S at 17).

features.name: Primal Strikes
features.description:
Flurry Techniques while wielding Primal Weapon:
  • Cross Synergy — on nature-release Ninjutsu cast, 2 Discipline: gain Chain keyword + Primal Chain effect (counts as Chain 1/2/3).
  • Elemental Augmentation — on an ally with Elemental condition ranks within 15 ft, 1 Discipline + 1 Flurry Die. 10+: all ranks removed; 7+: -2 ranks; else -1. Primal Weapon gains +1 Flurry Die damage (matching type or Force).
  • Disperse Chakra — on [Weapon Damage], 2 Discipline: 1d20 + 1 Flurry Die vs caster's save DC; success = Dispel one jutsu effect. No DC = use Standard Ability Check DC.

PRIMAL CHAIN (keyword)
Ninjutsu you cast that gain the Chain keyword (via Cross Synergy or otherwise) gain the associated Chain effect, regardless of the Chain rank used:
  • Earth Release — If your jutsu requires an attack, on a hit the affected creature makes a Strength saving throw. If your jutsu already requires a saving throw, apply this effect on a failed save. On a failed save you magnify their weight as they succumb to the intense gravity. Reduce the number of attacks they can make and the number of creatures they can target with their next jutsu or attack by 1 (Min. 1). Penalty increases by 1 (2 reduced attacks/targets) at 10th, and again at 17th (3 reduced attacks/targets at 17th).
  • Wind Release — If your jutsu requires an attack, on a hit the affected creature makes a Dexterity saving throw. If your jutsu already requires a saving throw, apply this effect on a failed save. On a failed save you release a burst of blinding wind overcoming them as they're thrown 30 feet, fall prone and suffer half the result of 1d12 as a penalty to their next attack before the beginning of your next turn. Increases to their next two attacks at 10th and next three attacks at 17th level.
  • Fire Release — If your jutsu requires an attack, on a hit the affected creature makes a Constitution saving throw. If your jutsu already requires a saving throw, apply this effect on a failed save. On a failed save you release a wave of flaming chakra overwhelming them as you drain them of their immediate fighting potential. They suffer a 1d12 penalty to their next damage roll. Increases to 2d12 at 10th and 3d12 at 17th level.
  • Water Release — If your jutsu requires an attack, on a hit the affected creature makes a Constitution saving throw. If your jutsu already requires a saving throw, apply this effect on a failed save. On a failed save you release a torrent of waves breaking your enemy and disorienting them as the next attack targeting them, until the end of your next turn, can be made at advantage. Increases to the next two attacks at 10th and the next three attacks at 17th level.
  • Lightning Release — If your jutsu requires an attack, on a hit the affected creature makes a Dexterity saving throw. If your jutsu already requires a saving throw, apply this effect on a failed save. On a failed save the affected creature is electrocuted and the next time they attempt to use their Action or Bonus action or at least 5 feet of movement they must roll a 1d12, losing the attempted action or movement if they roll 10 or more. The result needed to lose the attempted movement or action decreases to 8 or more at 10th level and 6 or more at 17th level.

features.name: Chained Cascade
features.description:
On C-rank-or-lower Ninjutsu as Action, 3 Discipline: cast a same-rank Bukijutsu at no additional action cost. Enter Chained Cascade state until end of next turn (extend by 1 round on re-trigger). → B-rank at 14.

CHAINED CASCADE (Battle Amp)
Your chakra surges outward in a relentless cascade. While in Chained Cascade:
  • Chained Empowerment. You gain a number of temporary Chakra equal to twice the Chain rank you benefitted from as part of the Bukijutsu cast with your Chained Cascade feature. These temporary chakra points dissipate when this state ends.
  • Cascading Chakra. You gain a rank of Mastery to your Ninjutsu attacks and a +1 bonus to your Ninjutsu save DC.
  • Protective Barrier. As a reaction to an allied creature within 10 feet of you taking damage you can transfer all remaining temporary hit points granted by Chained Cascade to them, allowing the transferred temporary hit points to be applied to the triggering damage.

actions.name: Protective Barrier
actions.description: While in Chained Cascade, when an allied creature within 10 ft takes damage, transfer all remaining temporary HP from this state to them, applying it to the triggering damage.

features.name: Resonant Jaunt
features.description: Special sense: detect elemental material/effects up to 1 mile away (can't penetrate chakra barriers). Out of combat, 1 min focus on an elemental source within sense range: teleport to within 10 ft; spend 1 Discipline per additional ally. Once per rest.

features.name: Primal Weapon Mastery
features.description:
Additional Flurry Techniques:
  • Elemental Mark — Bonus Action, 1 Discipline: mark a creature within 60 ft with an Elemental Mark (sensed as elemental effect). On jutsu cast by marked, AoO triggered — cast a known Ninjutsu in place of attack.
  • Primal break — on nature-release Bukijutsu matching your Primal Weapon, 2 Discipline: upcast at no additional chakra cost, ignoring rank limits.
  • Chakra Network Overload — on [Weapon Damage] with Primal Weapon, 3 Discipline: CON save; fail = chakra damage = [Weapon Damage] + Chakra Strike bonus (no additional Discipline); concentration checks at disadvantage.

features.name: Reflective Sunder
features.description: On successful Chakra Sunder clash, 1 additional Discipline: redirect effects back to caster. Attack → your Taijutsu attack on caster; Save → caster saves vs your Taijutsu DC.

features.name: Break/Reinforce the Seal
features.description:
On a Flurry Technique applied as part of [Weapon Damage] costing ≤4 Discipline, pay 4 Discipline to pick one:
  • Break the Seal — CON save; fail = for 1 min, double jutsu costs and halve damage dice. End-of-turn resave (cooldown: rest).
  • Reinforce the Seal — CON save; fail = for 1 min, can't cast jutsu >12 chakra or concentrate on >6 chakra. End-of-turn resave (cooldown: rest).

natureReleaseGrants.description: Select one nature release (Earth, Wind, Fire, Water, or Lightning). If you already have multiple releases, the picker excludes already-owned ones.

subclasses.name: Seeker
subclasses.description: Ranged-combat specialists who mark targets with Vector Seals — triangulating pinpoint attacks, creating distance, and mapping the battlefield with chakra-tuned senses.

features.name: Converging Vector
features.description:
Bonus Action, 1 Discipline: mark one creature within 90 ft with a Vector Seal (1 min, dispellable by D-rank Genjutsu dispel). One active. Benefits vs marked:
  • Always Enough — no Ammunition Die rolls required.
  • Triangulation — +1 to hit with ranged weapon/Taijutsu attacks (rank of Mastery instead if allies within 15 ft of target).
  • Course Correction — missed attack by ≤5, 1 Discipline: ricochet-remake vs same target (no crits).
  • Zeroing In — if target ends turn without moving 5+ ft, advantage on your next attack vs them.

features.name: Crossfire Aim
features.description:
Flurry Techniques with Ammunition or Thrown weapons:
  • Critical Aperture — on ranged crit, 2 Discipline: -1 target AC OR saves (-2 at 10) until next turn.
  • Snap Volley — after [Weapon Damage], 2 Discipline + Bonus Action: 2 ranged weapon attacks (same or different creatures) dealing 1 Flurry Die each (+1 attack at 10 and 17).
  • Threaded Relay — once/turn on an ally damaging a Vector Seal target, 1 Discipline: 1 ranged weapon attack dealing 1 Flurry Die.

features.name: Creating / Maintaining Distance
features.description:
Creating Distance — Reaction + 2 Discipline on a melee attack (not grabbed/prone/restrained): DEX save vs the attack; success = miss and move 20 ft away (no AoOs) and ranged attack.

Maintaining Distance — if you haven't moved 5+ ft since last turn and a Vector Seal creature exists, 2 Discipline: advantage on first ranged attack this turn vs them; rerolls 1s and 2s.

features.name: Vector Mapping
features.description: 1 min meditation: map a 200-ft radius for 1 hour — entrances/exits, corridors, shafts, doors, hazards (no specifics on what). Can't penetrate chakra barriers; doesn't reveal creatures/illusions/secret compartments. Time × 10 per use before rest.

features.name: Dread Ambusher
features.description:
Two Vector Seals at once (mark both same bonus action, no cost). Vector Seal now requires B-rank Genjutsu dispel.

Additional Flurry Techniques:
  • Siphoning Seal — on [Weapon Damage] to Seal target, 3 Discipline: all Seal targets -10 ft speed (no speed bonuses) until next turn.
  • Point Blank Shot — on ranged attack vs adjacent, 3 Discipline: no disadvantage + advantage; if Seal target, result 10+ over AC = crit.
  • Tethering Shot — on [Weapon Damage] to Seal target, 2 Discipline: other Seal target STR save; fail = both pulled 15 ft toward each other.

features.name: Sharpshooting Savant
features.description: First turn in combat (if not surprised): Action + X Discipline to attack X creatures within 90 ft that haven't acted yet. Once per turn on ranged attack, 2 Discipline: chakra rope — on hit, flip 20 ft away and STR save to pull target 20 ft toward you.

features.name: Seeker of the End
features.description: On hit vs Seal target, 3 Discipline: [Weapon Damage] becomes maximum. If this reduces a Standard/Elite target to ≤50 HP, CON save or drop to 0 instantly. Solos save at advantage.

subclasses.name: Reaver
subclasses.description: Blade/Power weapon specialists who trade space for inevitability — applying Reaver Echoes to Quarries for delayed-blast damage reduction and crowd control.

features.name: Reavers Quarry
features.description:
Bonus Action: mark a creature within 60 ft as your Quarry (1 min, one at a time). Mark renews on Quarry drop to 0 HP. Benefits:
  • +1 [Weapon Damage] vs Quarry (+2 at 10, +3 at 17).
  • Once/turn on [Weapon Damage] to Quarry: generate Reaver Echoes = rank cast (Weapon/D: 1, C: 2, B: 3, A: 4, S: 5). Dispel if undetonated by your next turn.

features.name: Kenjutsu
features.description:
Flurry Techniques with Blade or Power weapons:
  • Frenetic Counter — on a Reaver-Echoes creature damaging you or an ally within 30 ft, Reaction + 1 Discipline: detonate Echoes to reduce damage by dice rolled (free if the action would detonate them anyway).
  • New Moon — on [Weapon Damage] to 2+ creatures in the same jutsu and applying Echoes to a Quarry, 2 Discipline: 1 Echo on each affected creature (2 at 10, 3 at 17).
  • Sundering Detonation — on a Barrier-keyword jutsu intercepting [Weapon Damage], 1 Discipline: inflict 3 Echoes on the barrier (detonate on its next damage).

features.name: Circle of Protection
features.description: When you + 1+ allies within 20 ft take damage from the same source: Reaction + 3 Discipline. Move up to speed (end adjacent to an ally). Roll 2 Flurry Die and reduce triggering damage by result for willing allies within 10 ft of your new position.

features.name: Reaver's Instincts
features.description:
Social techniques:
  • Viper's Tongue — on Lie/Impersonate, 2 Discipline: +1 Flurry Die. Success = passes as genuine; beat DC by 5 = accepted as absolute truth.
  • Apex Glare — on Coerce/Gather Information, 2 Discipline: +1 Flurry Die. Success = Gather Information reduced to 1 hour; beat DC by 5 = 10 minutes.

features.name: Severance
features.description:
When a Reaver-Echoes creature spends a Bonus Action, Echoes detonate. When Echoes would detonate/dispel, 1 Discipline or Reaction to extend all Echoes' duration until your next turn.

Additional Flurry Techniques (Blade/Power):
  • Reaver Gambit — on attack vs Echoes target, 2 Discipline: dispel one Echo for advantage; double nat-equal = crit + regain spent Discipline.
  • Wounding Scar — on Echoes detonation, 3 Discipline: affected creatures -1 AC per detonated Echo until end of next turn.
  • Burst Echo — in place of attack, 2 Discipline: manifest an Echo at a point within 60 ft; all within 5 ft take 2d12 (weapon damage type) + gain an Echo.

features.name: Delayed Displacement
features.description: Action (or in place of an attack during Bukijutsu casting), 4 Discipline: all Reaver Echoes on hostiles within 30 ft detonate, then a single creature of your choice within that radius gains Echoes = number detonated.

features.name: Reavers Judgement
features.description: Two Quarries at once (mark both same bonus action). Twice/turn Echoes generation on [Weapon Damage] to either Quarry. On any Echoes detonation, regain 1 Discipline per echo detonated (max 3).

pickable.editorDescription: Weapon Specialist Katas — techniques and stances that shape your build. Use + Custom for homebrew or DM-approved entries.
pickable.pickerDescription: Katas are learned at levels 2 (initial) and +1 each at 5 / 8 / 11 / 14 / 17 (6 total by cap).

options.name: Disciplined Illusions
options.description: Once/turn on a Genjutsu cast, gain Discipline in place of a Bukijutsu cast: D/C = 1, B/A = 2, S = 3. Can't combine with Discipline-from-Chain or Disciplined Ninshou in the same turn.

options.name: Disciplined Maneuvers
options.description: Once/turn on a skill action (Trip / Grapple / Shove / Disarm / Maintain Focus / Feint / Demoralize / Read the Enemy): gain 1 Discipline (2 at 11, 3 at 20). Can't combine with Discipline-from-Chain or Disciplined Strikes in the same turn.

options.name: Disciplined Ninshou
options.description: Once/turn on a Ninjutsu cast, gain Discipline in place of a Bukijutsu cast: D/C = 1, B/A = 2, S = 3. Can't combine with Discipline-from-Chain or Disciplined Illusions in the same turn.

options.name: Disciplined Strikes
options.description: Once/turn on Attack action: gain 1 Discipline per attack. Multiattack/Light-property attacks don't count. Can't combine with Discipline-from-Chain or Disciplined Maneuvers in the same turn.

options.name: Efficient Bukijutsu
options.description: Once/turn on a Bukijutsu cast, 1 Discipline: reduce its cost by rank (D: 1, C: 2, B: 3, A: 4, S: 5).

options.name: Efficient Chain
options.description: Once/turn on a Chain Bukijutsu: skip chain effects but sustain current chain rank. Gain Discipline equal to the chain rank sustained.

options.name: Defensive Draw
options.description: On Deflection vs a ranged attack / effect from a non-adjacent hostile within 60 ft, 1 additional Discipline: redirect reduced damage to another creature within 30 ft. Ranged weapon attack vs them — on hit, [Weapon Damage] + the reduced damage.

options.name: Duelist Retribution
options.description: Once/turn on a melee hit against you, 2 Discipline: weapon attack vs triggering creature.

options.name: Enduring Fortitude
options.description: Once/turn as Reaction to being hit, 1 Discipline: rank of Mastery to AC vs triggering attack until end of current turn. Alternatively, on crit hit: 3 Discipline to downgrade crit to normal hit.

options.name: Infectious Determination
options.description: Once/turn on a passed save, 2 Discipline: allies who see/hear you gain rank of Mastery to their next save before the end of your or their next turn.

options.name: Persistent Will
options.description: On a concentration check from damage, 2 Discipline: add 1 Flurry Die. Success by 5+ = regain 1 Discipline; by 10+ = next concentration cost on one jutsu = 0.

options.name: Pressure Build
options.description: Each [Weapon Damage] inflicted: +1 DR until your next turn. AoE damage counts per-creature.

options.name: Riposte
options.description: Reaction on a damaging Taijutsu/Bukijutsu cast by a creature within 30 ft, 2 Discipline: cast a 1-Action Bukijutsu — triggers a Clash. Loser's jutsu is Interrupted (no penalties).

options.name: Battle Boast
options.description: Once/turn, in place of an attack or Bukijutsu, 2 Discipline: 20-ft cone WIS save. Fail = focus shifts to you (disadvantage on attacks not targeting you; allies gain Mastery on saves vs non-you targets).

options.name: Calming Strike
options.description: Once/turn on a hit as attack of opportunity, 2 Discipline: affected creature gains 3 ranks Slow until end of current turn.

options.name: Controlled Positioning
options.description: Once/turn on [Weapon Damage], up to 2 Discipline: push damaged creature 10 ft per Discipline spent (non-vertical).

options.name: Disorienting Strike
options.description: Once/turn on [Weapon Damage], 3 Discipline: WIS save. Fail = pick one for 1 min (end-of-turn resave): halved speed + can't react to Bukijutsu; -2 AC; or no bonus actions. Immunity per long rest after ending.

options.name: Executioner
options.description:
Once/turn on a Bukijutsu, 4 Discipline (can't gain Discipline from this Bukijutsu): on hit or failed save, pick one (long rest before reuse on same target):
  • Kill — Standard adversary at ≤50 HP instantly drops to 0.
  • Cripple — Standard/Elite at ≤50 HP: STR save or Staggered for 1 min.

options.name: Horrid Aggression
options.description: Bonus Action, 2 Discipline: afterimage acceleration. On first Attack-action [Weapon Damage] this turn, STR save — fail = inflict one of Bruised / Weakened / Dazzled / Concussed.

options.name: Muted Strike
options.description: Once/turn on [Weapon Damage], 2 Discipline: CON save — fail = cannot cast D-rank-or-lower jutsu until end of their next turn (C at 9, B at 17).

options.name: Overwhelming Malice
options.description: Action: enter Overflowing Malice stance until next turn. Once/turn in stance, 3 Discipline: Retributive Strike (melee: AoO-on-enter/exit, [Weapon Damage] + 1 Flurry Die, DEX save or prone) OR Pensive Shot (ranged: AoO-at-15-ft, 2 Flurry Die damage, DEX save or restrained).

options.name: Unbound Ferocity
options.description: Bonus Action, 3 Discipline: pick a hostile within 60 ft. Until next turn, advantage on Taijutsu attacks vs them; they gain advantage on attacks vs you.

options.name: Critical Movement
options.description: Once/turn on a crit, 2 Discipline: gain Dash at no additional action cost. Each creature you pass: one weapon attack (once per creature per activation).

options.name: Strider
options.description: 1 Discipline at turn start: ignore natural difficult terrain. 2 Discipline instead: ignore jutsu-imposed difficult terrain. Until next turn.

options.name: Forced Separation
options.description: Once/turn on a melee attack (hit or miss), 1 Discipline: target can't make AoOs against you until end of current turn.

options.name: Elite Focus
options.description:
Bonus Action, 4 Discipline: enter heightened focus (concentration as B-rank jutsu). Pick 2 benefits:
  • +20 ft speed.
  • +2 AC.
  • Advantage on one save of your choice.
  • Extra Action each turn (skill action / Dash / Disengage / single weapon attack).

Maintain concentration at start of each turn by spending 2 Discipline.

options.name: Trapper
options.description: 10 min + 2 Discipline: build a mundane trap (Chapter 5 rules) as if with a Trapper's Kit. Rank ≤ highest jutsu rank you know. With an actual kit, +1 Flurry Die to the crafting check.

options.name: Unfettered Attention
options.description: Bonus Action, 2 Discipline: target creature within 60 ft. For 1 min, they can't benefit from hidden/unseen vs you (within 120 ft).

options.name: Battle Medic
options.description: Once/turn, Bonus Action + 2 Discipline: target a willing creature and roll 2 Flurry Die. 10+ = remove 1 rank of any Physical condition; matching dice = remove all ranks. Usable twice per rest per creature.

options.name: Hidden Hand
options.description:
Rank of Mastery in Stealth. On Hide action with a Thrown weapon / explosive tool, pick one:
  • Tricky Escape — set the tool; 2 Discipline to detonate any time before your next turn without revealing your position.
  • Parting Gift — 2 Discipline: attach tool to Thrown weapon and attack (detonates on hit). Pre-Hide so no position reveal.
